programme.desc: British inspector Harry Sanders investigates diamond and gold fraud in Africa, uncovering a plot involving smugglers raiding a sunken ship. Action adventure sequel to Death Drums along the River, starring Richard Todd and Dale Robertson

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: A teenager meets her older self, who gives her valuable advice about her future

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.sub-title: An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge
programme.desc: The hanging of a spy goes wrong as the rope snaps, plunging him to freedom

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomons diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichtons novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov

programme.title: The Last Son (2021)
programme.desc: Isaac LeMay, a murderous outlaw, learns he is cursed by a prophecy - one of his children will kill him. To prevent this, he hunts down each of his estranged children, including long-lost son Cal. With bounty hunters and Sheriff Solomon on his tail, LeMay must find a way to stop his children and end the curse. Western, starring Sam Worthington, Heather Graham, Thomas Jane and Colson Baker, also known as Machine Gun Kelly

programme.title: Backtrace (2018)
programme.desc: After suffering a brain injury, the lone surviving thief of a botched bank heist is sprung from a high-security facility and given an experimental drug, which the authorities hope will rebuild his memory. However, he must also elude a detective and an FBI agent, as well as cope with the drugs side-effects in order to recover stolen cash. Director Brian A Millers action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Ryan Guzman and Matthew Modine.

programme.title: Lawnmower Man 2: Beyond Cyberspace (1995)
programme.desc: Computerised megalomaniac Jobe returns in a bid to take over the world from cyberspace, and only a youngster and a scientist stand in his way. Sci-fi sequel, starring Patrick Bergin, Matt Frewer and Austin OBrien

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: American Warships (2012)
programme.desc: Alien invaders try to wipe out the human race, and while modern military forces prove useless against the attackers, the crew of a Second World War-era US Navy battleship on the brink of being decommissioned proves mankinds only hope. Sci-fi adventure, starring Mario Van Peebles, Carl Weathers, Johanna Watts and Nikki McCauley

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: As violence erupts on a busy street, Romano gets caught in the crossfire and suffers a nasty head wound, while Hooker is accused of shooting an unarmed youngster. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner and Adrian Zmed

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: An ambitious detective tries to frame one of Hookers friends in connection with a gang of jewel thieves. Guest starring Jerry Lee Lewis

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A hearing-impaired 14-year-old gang member is conned into taking the rap for a murder committed by big-time drug dealer Carlos Vega. Vintage police drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: Romano and Hooker try to avert a war between independent hauliers and an unscrupulous wholesaler who has been sabotaging their trucks. Police drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: The cop is horrified to discover drug abuse is rife at a local high school. Police drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Buckskin (1968)
programme.desc: A crooked and wealthy rancher cuts off a towns water supply in a bid to drive the locals from their land. A tough-talking marshal arrives, determined to stand up to them - but he finds the residents arent too keen to lend him a hand. Run-of-the-mill Western, starring Barry Sullivan, Joan Caulfield, Lon Chaney Jnr and Wendell Corey

programme.title: Distant Drums (1951)
programme.desc: An army officer mounts a daring raid through the treacherous Florida swamplands. His mission is to take on a group of marauding Seminole rebels, as well as the gun runners who have been supplying them with weapons, and rescue a group of settlers who have been taken hostage by the tribe. Western, starring Gary Cooper, Mari Aldon, Richard Webb, Ray Teal and Arthur Hunnicutt

programme.title: The Gambler (1980)
programme.desc: Country singer Kenny Rogers plays a tough gambler in the old West who finds himself in serious danger after answering a plea for help. Christine Belford, Bruce Boxleitner and Harold Gould co-star

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: An inquisitive reporter tries to find the key to the apparent immortality of a glamorous movie star

programme.title: The Twilight Zone
programme.desc: An irritable taxi driver insults his TV repairman, and later sees scenes from his private life on his TV screen. Starring William Demarest, Joan Blondell and Sterling Holloway

programme.title: Mobsters (1991)
programme.desc: Notorious gangsters Bugsy Siegel and Lucky Luciano and their cronies make a daring bid to overthrow the dons of Twenties New York. Crime thriller, starring Christian Slater, Patrick Dempsey, Richard Grieco, Anthony Quinn, F Murray Abraham and Michael Gambon

programme.title: 12 Rounds (2009)
programme.desc: A New Orleans detectives girlfriend is kidnapped by a vengeful criminal mastermind, who sets him a sadistic challenge. To save her, the cop must successfully complete a series of 12 death-defying challenges that send him on a dangerous race through the city. Action thriller, with John Cena, Aidan Gillen and Ashley Scott

programme.title: First Kill (2017)
programme.desc: A desperate father is blackmailed into helping a criminal gang evade a corrupt police chief. Action thriller, starring Bruce Willis, Hayden Christensen and Ty Shelton

programme.title: Inside Out (2011)
programme.desc: After being released from prison, an ex-con is pulled back into his old world when his best friend is involved in an accidental shooting. Crime drama, starring Triple H

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Helen is trapped in her car after a crash on the A66, while golfer Michael has collapsed with a suspected cardiac arrest in Hertfordshire. Plus, 15-year-old Jamie is treated for a broken jaw and possible spinal injury after falling 10 feet

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside the AandE department of St Georges in south-west London. Carole, aged 45, is brought in after collapsing at home with chest pains. Tests reveal she has suffered a heart attack, so she undergoes an emergency procedure to unblock an artery. Philip, a 53-year-old psychiatrist, is rushed in following a seizure. Doctors suspect he might have suffered a stroke, but when it is revealed he was diagnosed with lung cancer several months earlier, registrar Chris believes it could be related. Finally, doctors treat 25-year-old Lucy, who is suffering severe headaches brought on by the build-up of excess spinal fluid in her head

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Oxfordshire, a motocross collision leaves 12-year-old-Lewis with a painful broken thighbone, and paraglider Lucasz is treated for multiple fractures after a crash on the Cumbrian coast. A stabbing incident leaves Paul needing urgent medical attention near Hartford in Cambridgeshire

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Will, 13, arrives by helicopter after a serious collision with someone during a game of baseball, while in the paediatric area, teenage parents Connor and Saffron have arrived with their six-month-old daughter Amira, concerned about the irregular heartbeat their GP spotted. Pizza delivery rider Chris, 20, is brought in to resus following a motorbike accident. Series following life inside the AandE department of St Georges Hospital, south-west London

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The campers visit Tyn-yr-Onnen farm on the edge of Snowdonia National Park, where hosts Len and Helen encourage them to embrace farm life, from feeding animals to picking mushrooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at The Peniarth Arms in Bryncrug, where host Susanne Fox is joined by her best friend and colleague Anwen Roberts, but a revelation raises some eyebrows

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Toby Rhys Davies is second to host at Apple Camping in Redberth, Pembrokeshire, where he hopes his unique take on glamping will impress his rivals. The UFO and aeroplane accommodation prove a hit with the guests - but come feedback time, theres just something missing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Garden House in Blagdon, Somerset, where host and chef Ryan Blackman is hoping his breakfast will wow his guests. But when morning comes around, Susanne and Anwen find their brekkie hard to stomach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners head to Ivy House in St Austell, Cornwall, home to James Earl. Susanne and Anwen marvel at the delightful views, but the lack of a lock is a key issue for Ryan and Sarah. At breakfast, Tobys PJs are on the menu but its the sausage thats under scrutiny for Susanne and Anwen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners meet one last time to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. Ryan and Sarah seem rather upset over cleanliness concerns and James wants to chew over some tough breakfast feedback. Some strong opinions are aired, and differences debated before the payments are revealed and a winner is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leigh from Kent and her nephew Sammy enlist Jasmine Harmans help finding a holiday home in Tenerife within their budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: House-hunters Tracey and Fran have been best friends since their schooldays, and theyve now decided to club together to buy a holiday home on the east coast of Fuerteventura. Property expert Danni Menzies lines up five options for the pairs budget of £250,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Horse-lovers Mel and James seek a holiday home in Mijas Pueblo in southern Spain. Laura Hamilton shows them the best of what their £180,000 budget can get them with five impressive properties, hoping that at least one will have them chomping at the bit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman jets off to Spains southern Costa Cálida to help a pair of house-hunters from Stirlingshire find their dream holiday home. With five grown-up children and five grandchildren between them, Joan and Andrew want a property the whole family can enjoy. With a budget of £80,000 to spend, Ben singles out five impressive properties for the pair to consider

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host of the week in Gloucestershire, IT manager Bea, serves up an international menu inspired by her travels. However, with confusion over her Cretan feta starter and a disastrous guessing game based on countries shes visited, will her guests appreciate her geographical-themed night?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Gloucestershire, wine connoisseur Adam plans an evening full of posh plonk, excellent etiquette and fine dining. However, with some rowdy guests and one in particular choosing to overshare about their wind problems, Adams got a challenge on his hands if he wants to maintain decorum. When his dessert falls foul of a custard catastrophe, he decides to pull out a less-than-riveting party trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cycling enthusiast Steve hosts the third dinner party of the week in Gloucestershire, hoping to impress his guests with a menu inspired by his travels

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night in Gloucestershire, shark-loving mum-of-four Lisa plans to give her guests a night of pure entertainment, aiming for lots of light-hearted fun after the previous nights of haute cuisine and wine chat. With a fishcake starter and ribs for the main, Lisas hoping that her back-to-basics food will bag her the grand. And an interpretive dance challenge catches everyone off-guard

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Gloucestershire, hairdresser Lorna hopes an American 50s diner-style menu will be a success. A basic melon ball starter and a cremated American platter main dont seem to have the desired effect on her guests, and a disastrous dessert takes things from bad to worse

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Ramsgate, Kent for the first visit of the week, where partners Tess and Pete hope their seaside home from home, Rooms@119, will prove a relaxing retreat. Things dont get off to the best start though, when Hillary and Mark discover some unfinished business in their room. In the morning, Pete turns his hand to cooking for six for the first time ever, but finds out the hard way that any waiting around results in lost points come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Laurain and Kerry are second to host at Stable Oak Cottages in Maidstone, Kent, where they are confident their self-catering accommodation will be a hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosts and dairy farmers Hillary and Mark are hoping to show their guests some high-end hospitality at Hedgerow Luxury Glamping in Clitheroe, Lancashire. But Natasha and Tanya are put off by the discovery of a rather creepy visitor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Antelope Inn, in Upavon, Wiltshire, run by Natasha, whos joined in the competition by friend and colleague Tanya. Some guests are underwhelmed by the decor, while Kerry and Laurain find themselves complaining over staining in their room. Over dinner, Natashas revelation about how she became a landlady leaves some of the guests questioning her credentials

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment days sees Tess and Pete deal with the fallout from their first-ever six-person breakfast, while Laurain and Kerry are keen to get the bottom of what they believe are filthy comments about their cleanliness. Mattress moans are also on the agenda, before the payments - and winner - are revealed

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Staff at St Georges Hospital tackle one of their most dramatic emergencies when 60-year-old rail worker Alan is airlifted to the helipad after being hit by a high-speed train. A CT scan reveals he has suffered multiple fractures and loss of blood flow to his right arm. Without circulation he will lose the limb, so the medical team tries everything to save it, including complicated surgery. Meanwhile, 95-year-old Betty is brought in after experiencing breathing difficulties at home, and retired engineer James, 63, has a nasty cut to his head and weakness in his arms following a tumble down the stairs

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St Georges after suffering a seizure and losing consciousness, while 22-year-old Felix is brought to AandE with a self-inflicted stab wound to his stomach. Retired engineer Bill, 68, fell 15ft from a ladder while cutting a leylandii hedge. He arrives by air ambulance with breathing difficulties and suspected multiple fractures

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Tree surgeon David, 27, is airlifted to St Georges after falling 40ft to the ground, hitting several branches on his way down and landing head-first onto concrete. Having been knocked unconscious for 10 minutes, the medical team must urgently assess him for internal injuries. Anne, 74, arrives with a sharp pain in her chest, 51-year-old taxi driver Mark receives treatment for a cut on his finger caused by broken glass, and 92-year-old Betty is brought in by ambulance after her carers found her with slurred speech and a shaking hand

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Cameras capture the work of some of Britains busiest air ambulance teams as they race to save lives. The helicopter medics cut short an appearance at a charity event in Suffolk to treat a dirtbiker whos crashed at speed. Sammy has a cardiac arrest while working at a windfarm, and Stephen breaks his thigh while off-road motorbiking in the Pennines

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Staff at St Georges Hospital tackle one of their most dramatic emergencies when 60-year-old rail worker Alan is airlifted to the helipad after being hit by a high-speed train. A CT scan reveals he has suffered multiple fractures and loss of blood flow to his right arm. Without circulation he will lose the limb, so the medical team tries everything to save it, including complicated surgery. Meanwhile, 95-year-old Betty is brought in after experiencing breathing difficulties at home, and retired engineer James, 63, has a nasty cut to his head and weakness in his arms following a tumble down the stairs

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St Georges after suffering a seizure and losing consciousness, while 22-year-old Felix is brought to AandE with a self-inflicted stab wound to his stomach. Retired engineer Bill, 68, fell 15ft from a ladder while cutting a leylandii hedge. He arrives by air ambulance with breathing difficulties and suspected multiple fractures

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: The camping critics penultimate stop is at Mynydd Derwydd, a wild camping site on the edge of Clocaenog Forest in south-west Wales. With 250 acres of stunning scenery to explore, but next to no facilities, campsite owner Llyr tries to win them over with a bushcraft skills workshop, a lamb barbecue and a breakfast delivery of fresh farm produce

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dressage champion Natasha Baker is the first of five Paralympic athletes hosting dinner parties, serving up an evening of home-cooked food and old-fashioned family entertainment to judoka Chris Skelley, sports presenter Arthur Williams, wheelchair basketball player Amy Conroy and retired swimmer Liz Johnson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Paralympic judoka Chris Skelley gets to grips with the pots and pans on the second night of the dinner party challenge. Wheelchair basketball star Amy Conroy is confused by his menu, although retired swimmer Liz Johnson is impressed with the starter. The entire group are all blown away by Chriss motivation for a medal in Tokyo, while his karaoke performance is memorable for entirely different reasons

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hopes are high for Paralympic presenter Arthur Williams as he serves up an international menu - fondue from Switzerland, nasi goreng from the Far East and apple crumble from the UK

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wheelchair basketball star Amy Conroy fears that her simple menu is not going to be enough to grab the £1,000 prize, so ups the ante with a night fit for historical heroes. Its a fun evening as Amy welcomes her guests dressed as Cleopatra and first through the door is Arthur as Einstein, followed by Chris claiming to be Julius Caesar. Bringing a bit of style to the evening is Natasha, who comes as Audrey Hepburn, and finally Liz, whos opted to come as a suffragette. Will Amys night of history and beer pong be enough to overcome a beige menu and clinch the cash?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host of the week is retired swimmer Liz Johnson, whos hoping that her menu of personal favourites will win her the £1,000 prize for charity. Without exception, Lizs guests embrace her onesie dress code, especially Chris who is bear-ly recognisable on arrival. Natasha isnt a fan of the fishy starter, but Arthur and Chris bond over her leftovers - a sight that touches the heart strings of Amy. The group swaps tales of previous games over paella and their competitive spirit is unleashed over a kazoo competition

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and thats close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lee Juggurnauth heads to Los Gigantes, Tenerife to meet ambulance dispatcher Helen and builder Andrew from Yorkshire who are looking for a holiday home. Armed with a budget of £130,000 , Lee shows the couple five properties with hopes that one of them will be their dream holiday home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Carol are looking for a holiday home in the south-west of Tenerife. Property expert Danni Menzies shows them five properties she trusts will prove suitable within their £120,000 budget

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help Rachael and Tim, who cannot decide to do with their home in Wandsworth, south London. New Jersey-born Rachael wants to up sticks and move to the countryside to bring up their children in more rural surroundings, and she is finding their four-bedroom semi something of a squeeze. However, local lad Tim would rather stay closer to his friends and family. Kirstie comes up with a plan to make better use of the space, while Phil aims to show the couple three properties that prove the grass is greener outside the M25

programme.title: George Clarkes Remarkable Renovations
programme.desc: Rollo and Olivia have decided to swap city life for the rolling Chiltern Hills, snapping up an old barbers shop and its attached 17th-century building in Amersham. Run as a business for over 100 years, the Grade II listed hairdressers is in a state of serious disrepair. Their plan to convert the loft and create a modern open-plan ground floor is perfect for modern family living, but its a risk in a building thats over 300 years old

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kim is looking for a holiday home on Spains eastern Costa del Sol and shes brought her younger sister Debbie along to help. Both Kim and Debbie grew up by the sea in south Wales, so they desperately want their holiday home to be within a stones throw of a beach. Leah Charles-King shows them what their £115,000 can get as she lines up five properties for them to visit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Whitney and Rose have been married for eight years and dream about having their own holiday home around La Zenia on the Spanish southern Costa Blanca, where they can enjoy an all-year-round warm climate. Now, they feel the time is finally right for them to make their dream a reality and theyre putting all their hopes in property expert Ben Hillman to find them something suitable on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition starts at the Mevagissey Bay Hotel in Cornwall, where owners John and Elleke Ross hope that the stunning sea views will live up to their premium price tag. Guests Sarah and Jay savour the sights, while Catherine and Andrew are dazzled by the decking but puzzled by a mysterious gap around their door. During the afternoon fishing trip, John is angling to outdo solo contestant Sarah, but her take on the facilities leaves the hosts all at sea. In the morning, Catherine and Andrew get a rude awakening, and the breakfast pre-order system gives the other guests food for thought

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week takes place at Glebe House Cottages in Devon, which is owned by Jay and Sarah Brewer

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Kings Park Hotel in Retford, Nottinghamshire, which hosts Catherine and Andrew Watt converted from a former police station

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Dukes in Heath and Reach, Bedfordshire, and while owner Sarah Smith impresses the guests at dinner, the pressure gets to her at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the BandB owners meet for the last time to find out what theyve been paid, while also settling some scores

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: A woman who is 29 weeks pregnant is semi-conscious with a suspected blood clot in her lungs. Dr Scott fears that she could lose her baby and her life. Meanwhile, Dr Luke races against time to save a students arm after a machete attack left a deep wound, and Dr Mya examines an injured mountain biker who took a jump on a country trail and flew eight metres before landing hard

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Peter, 85, has been brought - unwillingly - to AandE by his stepson Brian. He cut his finger on some wire two weeks earlier and his hand is swollen and wont stop bleeding. Emma, 23, is rushed to St Georges after crashing her car on the way to her fathers house, while doctors also see to 86-year-old Harry, who fell down a flight of stairs a day earlier but didnt seek treatment. Ciaran, 27, injured his ankle the previous night while out with friends and was given a temporary cast. Emergency nurse practitioner Helen needs to get him X-rayed and replastered, but he faces a return visit for an operation

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 68-year-old professional actor visits St Georges Hospital after injuring his shoulder while rushing to catch a train, and a check-up indicates he may need corrective surgery to set things right, but further assessment suggests he might be living with a much more serious condition. An octogenarian couple are rushed in to AandE after being knocked over by a motorcyclist while crossing the road, and the police maintain an active presence as paramedics bring in a man who has collapsed after a suspected drug overdose. Plus, an 88-year-old woman with a swollen leg discusses her ill-fated marriage to a much younger man nearly four decades ago

programme.desc: Horse-lovers Mel and James seek a holiday home in Mijas Pueblo in southern Spain. Laura Hamilton shows them the best of what their £180,000 budget can get them with five impressive properties, hoping that at least one will have them chomping at the bit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman jets off to Spains southern Costa Cálida to help a pair of house-hunters from Stirlingshire find their dream holiday home. With five grown-up children and five grandchildren between them, Joan and Andrew want a property the whole family can enjoy. With a budget of £80,000 to spend, Ben singles out five impressive properties for the pair to consider

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host of the week in Gloucestershire, IT manager Bea, serves up an international menu inspired by her travels. However, with confusion over her Cretan feta starter and a disastrous guessing game based on countries shes visited, will her guests appreciate her geographical-themed night?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Gloucestershire, wine connoisseur Adam plans an evening full of posh plonk, excellent etiquette and fine dining. However, with some rowdy guests and one in particular choosing to overshare about their wind problems, Adams got a challenge on his hands if he wants to maintain decorum. When his dessert falls foul of a custard catastrophe, he decides to pull out a less-than-riveting party trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cycling enthusiast Steve hosts the third dinner party of the week in Gloucestershire, hoping to impress his guests with a menu inspired by his travels

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night in Gloucestershire, shark-loving mum-of-four Lisa plans to give her guests a night of pure entertainment, aiming for lots of light-hearted fun after the previous nights of haute cuisine and wine chat. With a fishcake starter and ribs for the main, Lisas hoping that her back-to-basics food will bag her the grand. And an interpretive dance challenge catches everyone off-guard

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Gloucestershire, hairdresser Lorna hopes an American 50s diner-style menu will be a success. A basic melon ball starter and a cremated American platter main dont seem to have the desired effect on her guests, and a disastrous dessert takes things from bad to worse

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Ramsgate, Kent for the first visit of the week, where partners Tess and Pete hope their seaside home from home, Rooms@119, will prove a relaxing retreat. Things dont get off to the best start though, when Hillary and Mark discover some unfinished business in their room. In the morning, Pete turns his hand to cooking for six for the first time ever, but finds out the hard way that any waiting around results in lost points come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Laurain and Kerry are second to host at Stable Oak Cottages in Maidstone, Kent, where they are confident their self-catering accommodation will be a hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosts and dairy farmers Hillary and Mark are hoping to show their guests some high-end hospitality at Hedgerow Luxury Glamping in Clitheroe, Lancashire. But Natasha and Tanya are put off by the discovery of a rather creepy visitor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Antelope Inn, in Upavon, Wiltshire, run by Natasha, whos joined in the competition by friend and colleague Tanya. Some guests are underwhelmed by the decor, while Kerry and Laurain find themselves complaining over staining in their room. Over dinner, Natashas revelation about how she became a landlady leaves some of the guests questioning her credentials

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment days sees Tess and Pete deal with the fallout from their first-ever six-person breakfast, while Laurain and Kerry are keen to get the bottom of what they believe are filthy comments about their cleanliness. Mattress moans are also on the agenda, before the payments - and winner - are revealed

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Staff at St Georges Hospital tackle one of their most dramatic emergencies when 60-year-old rail worker Alan is airlifted to the helipad after being hit by a high-speed train. A CT scan reveals he has suffered multiple fractures and loss of blood flow to his right arm. Without circulation he will lose the limb, so the medical team tries everything to save it, including complicated surgery. Meanwhile, 95-year-old Betty is brought in after experiencing breathing difficulties at home, and retired engineer James, 63, has a nasty cut to his head and weakness in his arms following a tumble down the stairs

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St Georges after suffering a seizure and losing consciousness, while 22-year-old Felix is brought to AandE with a self-inflicted stab wound to his stomach. Retired engineer Bill, 68, fell 15ft from a ladder while cutting a leylandii hedge. He arrives by air ambulance with breathing difficulties and suspected multiple fractures

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Helen is trapped in her car after a crash on the A66, while golfer Michael has collapsed with a suspected cardiac arrest in Hertfordshire. Plus, 15-year-old Jamie is treated for a broken jaw and possible spinal injury after falling 10 feet

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: A woman who is 29 weeks pregnant is semi-conscious with a suspected blood clot in her lungs. Dr Scott fears that she could lose her baby and her life. Meanwhile, Dr Luke races against time to save a students arm after a machete attack left a deep wound, and Dr Mya examines an injured mountain biker who took a jump on a country trail and flew eight metres before landing hard

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dressage champion Natasha Baker is the first of five Paralympic athletes hosting dinner parties, serving up an evening of home-cooked food and old-fashioned family entertainment to judoka Chris Skelley, sports presenter Arthur Williams, wheelchair basketball player Amy Conroy and retired swimmer Liz Johnson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Paralympic judoka Chris Skelley gets to grips with the pots and pans on the second night of the dinner party challenge. Wheelchair basketball star Amy Conroy is confused by his menu, although retired swimmer Liz Johnson is impressed with the starter. The entire group are all blown away by Chriss motivation for a medal in Tokyo, while his karaoke performance is memorable for entirely different reasons

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hopes are high for Paralympic presenter Arthur Williams as he serves up an international menu - fondue from Switzerland, nasi goreng from the Far East and apple crumble from the UK

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wheelchair basketball star Amy Conroy fears that her simple menu is not going to be enough to grab the £1,000 prize, so ups the ante with a night fit for historical heroes. Its a fun evening as Amy welcomes her guests dressed as Cleopatra and first through the door is Arthur as Einstein, followed by Chris claiming to be Julius Caesar. Bringing a bit of style to the evening is Natasha, who comes as Audrey Hepburn, and finally Liz, whos opted to come as a suffragette. Will Amys night of history and beer pong be enough to overcome a beige menu and clinch the cash?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host of the week is retired swimmer Liz Johnson, whos hoping that her menu of personal favourites will win her the £1,000 prize for charity. Without exception, Lizs guests embrace her onesie dress code, especially Chris who is bear-ly recognisable on arrival. Natasha isnt a fan of the fishy starter, but Arthur and Chris bond over her leftovers - a sight that touches the heart strings of Amy. The group swaps tales of previous games over paella and their competitive spirit is unleashed over a kazoo competition

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and thats close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

However, local lad Tim would rather stay closer to his friends and family. programme.desc: Rollo and Olivia have decided to swap city life for the rolling Chiltern Hills, snapping up an old barbers shop and its attached 17th-century building in Amersham. Run as a business for over 100 years, the Grade II listed hairdressers is in a state of serious disrepair. Their plan to convert the loft and create a modern open-plan ground floor is perfect for modern family living, but its a risk in a building thats over 300 years old

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kim is looking for a holiday home on Spains eastern Costa del Sol and shes brought her younger sister Debbie along to help. programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: A look at one of Britains worst air disasters - the crash of British European Airways 548, which saw 118 people lose their lives. The plane plummeted shortly after taking off from Heathrow on June 18, 1972 and narrowly missed a main road before crashing into a field. Investigators were forced to rely on witness testimony to determine why the plane nosedived as there was no cockpit voice recorder on board, but both the investigation process and its results were deemed controversial

programme.title: Flintoff
programme.desc: In December 2022, Andrew Freddie Flintoff sustained severe injuries while filming at the Dunsfold Aerodrome in Surrey for an episode of Top Gear. He disappeared from public view to begin his rehabilitation and later said that he felt lucky to be alive. Award-winning filmmaker John Dower follows Flintoff over the course of 12 months to document his recovery and the impact on his life away from the spotlight. The feature-length film invites the former international cricketer and broadcast presenter to reflect on his sporting career, which including two Ashes series wins with England

programme.title: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: A boar needs its tusks trimmed, and two reindeer vital to the survival of their herd are treated for serious injuries. Plus, Dr Oakley also assists some police dogs

programme.title: Who Killed Ivan the Terrible?
programme.desc: Criminologist David Wilson investigates the mysterious death of Russias first dictator, beginning by analysing rumours that the man who used terror to rule his huge empire may have been strangled by enemies in his court. The trail takes Wilson across Russia and back to London, where he explores a story of intrigue surrounding Englands Queen Elizabeth I, uncovering clues along the way which lead to a final twist in the tale

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes
programme.desc: Maiya May learns about the severe impacts of droughts and floods on agriculture and the alarming depletion of groundwater reserves. Despite these challenges, Maiya finds hope in innovative agricultural practices

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to visit some of the many islands that lay off the coastline. Heading to Foula and Fair Isle - the most remote island communities in Britain - he makes a nerve-racking visit to Da Snekk o da Smallie - a pothole in the cliffs of Foula that leads through to the Atlantic shore on the island, and learns about the beautiful knitting patterns that have made Fair Isle famous around the word

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the origins of Britain and its people, a story forged over thousands of years. In south Wales, he joins a team of archaeologists to examine 8,000-year-old footprints revealed beneath tidal mud banks, and abseils into a cave dating back 33,000 years. The historian also reveals how, as the last ice age receded some 11,000 years ago, Britain became an island, a fate that was sealed following a cataclysmic tsunami around 6,000 BC

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines what impact the arrival of farming had on ancient social structures. He dives off the Isle of Wight where archaeologists have found an 8,000-year-old hunter settlement and learns what happened when hunter-gatherers met farmers for the first time. The historian reveals that when cultivation eventually came to Britain around 4,000 BC, it brought about the Neolithic era and a deep set of changes in peoples way of life, from ground-breaking inventions like pottery, to new belief systems

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver delves into the mysteries of the Stone Age and the meaning of the sacred monuments that were built throughout Britain and Ireland. He begins by visiting a 6,000-year-old axe factory in the Lake District to explore how the use of stone shaped a belief system that included the cosmos for the first time. The historian also joins people arriving in Stonehenge to celebrate the mid-summer sunrise, and learns about the religious and sociological significance of the monument

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines the consequences of the application of metal to everyday life. He travels to the south-west of Ireland in search of the first copper to be mined beyond mainland Europe. He also handles the possessions of the Amesbury Archer, which contained earliest copper and gold ever found in Britain, putting the islands with their rich natural resources at the technological forefront of the Bronze Age

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to visit some of the many islands that lay off the coastline. The trail takes Wilson across Russia and back to London, where he explores a story of intrigue surrounding Englands Queen Elizabeth I, uncovering clues along the way which lead to a final twist in the tale

programme.title: The Anti-Soviet Fighters
programme.desc: The resistance of a handful of Lithuanian, Latvian and Estonian partisans against the Soviet Unions stranglehold on their territory at the end of the Second World War

programme.title: Iron Curtain: Living Under Soviet Occupation
programme.desc: In 1945, as his Red Army advanced towards Berlin to liberate Europe from Nazism, Stalin pursued another goal - to join the countries of Eastern Europe to his empire. In just a few years he succeeded in imposing the Soviet totalitarian model with an iron fist across Poland, Hungary, East Germany and Czechoslovakia. Citizens of those nations tell their stories of how their countries were occupied and transformed into communist states

programme.title: Iron Curtain: Living Under Soviet Occupation
programme.desc: In December 1949, Stalins empire celebrated its leaders 70th birthday. Soviet domination of Eastern European countries was total and will remain so until the dictators death in 1953

programme.title: Iron Curtain: Living Under Soviet Occupation
programme.desc: After Stalins death, the countries of the Eastern Bloc hoped for the end of communist dictatorship. Revolts spread, but Moscows grip was still strong and insurrections were brutally suppressed. The construction of the Berlin Wall in 1961 - physically dividing the German capital - stifled any hope of change

programme.title: Great Forts of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Exploring the epic battles, intricate designs and world-altering legacies of the great fortresses in the Mediterranean. Featuring Crusader forts in Rhodes, Malta and the Levant, Ottoman forts in Greece, Venetian forts in Crete, Moorish forts in Spain, Portugal, Morocco and Algeria, and Portuguese and Spanish forts on the North African coast

programme.desc: Paul Murton turns his attention to the countrys landlocked islands. He travels to Loch Lomond to get a birds-eye view of an iconic beauty spot, before gaining an insight into the lives of its residents

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the origins of ancient Britain and its people. He dives for 3,000-year-old treasure and potholes through an ancient copper mine to discover how a golden age of bronze gave way to a period of social and economic crisis and sharp climate change, eventually to be replaced by a new era of iron

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the age of Celtic Britain, a time of warriors, druids and kings of unimaginable wealth. He learns about a celebrated warrior from 300BC, the owner of a remarkable Iron Age sword, and tries his hand at divination as he explores the power of Celtic priests

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver charts the brutal battles that marked the beginning of Roman Britain, discovering evidence of the empires artillery and its first roads and forts. He examines the rebellion led by Queen Boudicca, which resulted in the deaths of thousands of Romans in St Albans, London and Colchester, and learns more about the creation of Hadrians Wall

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver concludes his journey through ancient history by focusing on the legacy of the Romans. He digs beneath a London tower block, examines building work from a large stadium and investigates the remains of an African woman who lived in York 1,800 years ago

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Islands in Loch Lomond: Landlocked Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton turns his attention to the countrys landlocked islands. He travels to Loch Lomond to get a birds-eye view of an iconic beauty spot, before gaining an insight into the lives of its residents

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver concludes his journey through ancient history by focusing on the legacy of the Romans. He digs beneath a London tower block, examines building work from a large stadium and investigates the remains of an African woman who lived in York 1,800 years ago

programme.title: King Arthurs Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones, Suzannah Lipscomb and Rob Bell take a fresh look at the Great Fire of London in 1666 by walking the actual route the fire took across the city, hour-by-hour and street-by-street, hoping to uncover what really happened during Britains worst inferno. In this first edition, the three presenters investigate how the fire started, heading to Pudding Lane, site of the bakery where the blaze broke out. Dan and historian Dorian Gerhold then place a temporary blue plaque on the very spot where the fire began

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: The team continues to walk the path of the infamous inferno, uncovering what really happened on the worst day of the four-day rampage. Dan Jones charts the destruction of the citys buildings, including Londons spiritual home St Pauls Cathedral, which burned so fiercely its stones exploded. Suzannah Lipscomb reveals how Pudding Lane baker Thomas Farriner made sure no blame landed on him and Rob Bell finds out how the fire moved so fast

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Documentary following the last day of the fires rampage through the city of London in 1666, and the after-effects that changed the capital city for ever. The team investigate the treatment for burns victims, and how authorities dealt with the 100,000 refugees made homeless. Finally, they walk through some of the streets where the fires impact still endures, and look at how London was rebuilt from the ashes

programme.title: King Arthurs Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur

programme.title: The Truth About Franco: Spains Forgotten Dictatorship
programme.desc: An examination of dictator Francisco Francos reign, beginning with a look at his rise to power after launching a coup in 1936 that led to the Spanish Civil War

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi faces the task of restoring a rare 17th-century painting of Queen Henrietta Maria, the wife of Charles I, while mechanics maestro Steve Fletcher has a fab fix when he is given a toy Yellow Submarine belonging to Hannah Clive, whose actor father John Clive provided the voice of John Lennon in the Beatles animation of the same name. Also, a woman brings in a vintage sewing machine that bonds her to the seamstress grandmother she never met, and Matt Nickels works on a splintered stained-glass window

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A painting depicting the Lebanese city of Beirut arrives at the barn. It was painted by Nonny Jackson-Twists grandfather just after the Second World War. He would very much like to hang the painting in his home, but its faded, the canvas cracked and in some sections the paint is flaking away. However, fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi is only too happy to take on this monumental task. Also being restored are pair of silver Shabbat candlesticks and a dilapidated car jack. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper finds out about British workhouses and Arusha Irvine learns the history of Victorian toilets as the pair continue their antiques search across Dundee and Yorkshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine finds herself with home advantage as shes joined by David Harper on the west coast of Scotland for the latest leg of their trip. After Roos head gets stuck in an antique she decides to stick with what she knows best - old glass. David examines a rare personal collection that tells the story of a once booming industry all antiques fans should know about

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: For their last outing, Arusha Irvine and David Harper enjoy a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other - and the elements - in a sporty classic car. Davids hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the final showdown Roo gambles on a Georgian long case clock. The duo then head to North Shields for the auction

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Cathy Hodd has a remote-control model battleship built from scratch for her by her grandfather. She turns to model expert David Burville to help restore it to its former glory. Other items seeking some much-needed attention include a Victorian garden watering syringe and an electrical meter that belonged to someone dear. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Silversmith Brenton West repairs a boxing trophy won by an accomplished sportsman and much-loved grandfather. Larry Gaines fought professionally from 1923 and refused to let prejudice hamper his career. He was the first black boxer to fight in the Royal Albert Hall and his family is immensely proud of him. Also being restored are a pair of old football boots and a vintage globe. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sarah Readings presents a very well-loved and very well-travelled companion in need of help from soft toy experts Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell. This little doll of a sailor was named Fairstar after the ship he was bought on, and the ship that carried Sarah and her parents to Australia in the 1960s as Ten Pound Poms - people encouraged to emigrate to Australia by the government with cheap fares. A mid-century camera and a childs musical chair are also rejuvenated

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Alex and his son Flynn bring in their cherished stuffed toy lamb for the attention of soft toy specialists Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell. The toy was given to Alex when he was just four months old and after a lifetime of cuddling it has lost an eye, an ear and is less squishy than they would like. Next in is Alejandro with his Great Grandmothers breadcrumb maker that is over 100-years-old and has been passed through generations of his family. Years of use has left the machine unusable but with any luck, metal expert Dominic Chinea will be able to bring it back to life. Mark Stuckey gets to grips with a vintage radiogram thats been silent for years. Owner Sue remembers her late father tapping his toes to the sounds of Frank Sinatra and Glenn Miller and wants to be able to share that joy with her grandson Zayn

programme.title: Ros na Rún
programme.desc: Berni gets the surprise of her life when Evan arrives back from Australia. John Joe, worried that Dee may leave Ros na Rún with Bláithín, again finds a way to ensure this does not happen

programme.title: Young MasterChef
programme.desc: Its the last of the Young MasterChef heats and the final five rookie cooks arrive at the kitchen to make themselves known to judges Poppy OToole and Kerth Gumbs. Tonight features a waiter from Gloucester, a law student from Nottingham, a mum from Burnley, an acting student from Leintwardine and an engineering student from Portsmouth bringing flavours of Mozambique

programme.title: Young MasterChef
programme.desc: The final nine cooks take on Pizza Night, banding together to keep their top-of-the-range ovens lit and fired up for making pizzas with flavours of their own design

programme.desc: National and international news

programme.title: Cheap European Homes
programme.desc: Retired cardiac nurse and psychotherapist Nuala, from Wexford, is embracing the next chapter of her life by searching for a home near the Canal du Midi in Beziers in France. She has spent most of her years travelling through France in her camper van and now hopes to establish a permanent base where her family, especially her grandchildren, can share in her love of French culture

programme.title: The Money List
programme.desc: Shane from Galway and Rachel from Offaly take on Karen from Donegal and Conor from Dublin. The winners will go on to face new challengers Ruben from Limerick and Laura from Kildare. Hosted by Baz Ashmawy

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hi-tech hotel in the self-governing Chinese gambling capital of Macau. Monica gets special access to a secret set of VIP suites dubbed The Mansion, where it takes more than just money to book an exclusive room. Giles visits a special theatre that sends its audience flying through the air, and helps to arrange the flowers for the Chinese New Year celebrations

programme.title: Realtaí na Gaeltachta
programme.desc: As the students get used to life in Rann na Feirste, Gaelic footballer turned Australian Football League player Oisín Mullin joins the group. Múinteori Niall lays down the law in the classroom at the Coláiste, but a spot of kayaking in the lake isnt plain sailing for everyone. While Des and Oisín get in a round of golf at Cruit, Amanda spends some therapeutic time with artist Morag - before the whole group get to show off their moves at a Hawaiian ceilí. In Irish

programme.desc: Jill Leavy trades her Dublin home hunt for a bold renovation, transforming her dads abandoned 1932 primary school in Coralstown into her first home

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith

programme.desc: Comedy and entertainment from the Tipperary duo, joined each evening by some of their well-known friends for a host of shenanigans

programme.title: The Gone
programme.desc: When a trashed car and abandoned phone are found near the hut Aileen disappeared from, the detectives realise that she was with someone else at the hut before she was attacked and fled. Plus, Richter struggles with his complicated feelings for Aileen and another Irish person goes missing

programme.title: Best Interests
programme.desc: Drama, starring Sharon Horgan and Michael Sheen as a couple driven apart over the decision whether to stop care for their daughters deteriorating health. After a late-night emergency involving a chest infection, medical staff decide that the treatment is too invasive and actively causing pain and long-term damage. Her parents resolve to fight for the right to keep her alive no matter the cost, but tension soon grows in the family

programme.desc: Hyacinth forces Richard to participate in a trip to the countryside to look for Iron Age remains, but further cultural excursions are curtailed when Daisy and Onslow report that Rose is behaving in an outrageous fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: Hearing from a young Kerry man who has taken on a series of adventure challenges in some of the most remote and inaccessible places on Earth. Presented by Anne Cassin

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Grace Pearson is found dead at a local festival. Detective Inspector Sam Gillespie - to the bewilderment of newly arrived Detective Sergeant Felix Livingstone - uses Sister Boniface as his scientific adviser. She determines that Pearson died by suffocation after her head was pushed into sawdust. The investigation leads to the three Bellamy siblings, at whose home Pearson was a housemaid. Crime drama, starring Lorna Watson and Max Brown, with Belinda Lang and Robert Daws

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: After her wartime friend goes missing in London, Frankie must work with Agatha Christie (guest star Honeysuckle Weeks) to uncover the reason behind the disappearance. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.title: For Peats Sake
programme.desc: The vital role turf has played in Ireland and the battle that ensued when the EU tried to stop Irish people cutting turf. For thousands of years people in Ireland have cut turf to heat their homes and cook their food. It was an accessible, affordable and vital fuel, made from the very soil of Ireland itself. Then in the 1990s people realised that there was a lot more to bogs than cheap fuel

programme.title: RTÉ News: One OClock and Weather
Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges examine Finbarr and Gareths two-storey brick house in Dublin, Erica and Liams new-build bungalow in County Mayo, and Laoises turret-style home in Galway

programme.title: The Hardacres
programme.desc: Mary is excited to join the charitable ladies circle on an outing to one of their philanthropical endeavours, which Ma decides to gatecrash, but the pair come face to face with a place that holds painful memories from their past. programme.title: The Great House Revival
programme.desc: After 18 years in Aarons mothers house, Aileen and Aaron sell up to renovate a spacious Victorian townhouse in New Ross. However, a tight budget, DIY challenges, and the early return of Aarons mother Joan puts pressure on the project

programme.title: Leonard and Hungry Paul
programme.desc: After a successful first date Leonard is head over heels for Shelley, though is unnerved when he hears she is due to go to the cinema with a man called Patrick. After obsessively questioning who he might be, Leonard knows he must ask Shelley outright. Elsewhere, Hungry Paul has been shortlisted for the e-mail sign-off phrase competition and attends the awards ceremony with his family, leading to a new opportunity for him with the National Mime Association. Comedy, narrated by Julia Roberts and starring Alex Lawther and Laurie Kynaston

programme.title: The Tommy Tiernan Show
programme.desc: Chat show in which neither the presenter nor the viewers are told the identity of the guests in advance. With the comedian in the dark about who he will be interviewing until they walk out to greet him, anything can happen - especially if Tommy doesnt have a clue who they are

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The Edinburgh detective investigates the murder of notorious gangster Morris Caffertys illegitimate son, as the search goes on to find the person who put Sammy in a life-threatening coma. The hunt for the killer is complicated by apparent links to a spate of hate crimes by racist extremists in the city centre. Gritty crime thriller, starring John Hannah and James Cosmo

programme.title: Wonders of the Moon with Dara O Briain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dara explores the ways in which the moon shapes many things on Earth and why there is a new race to get humans back to its surface. He meets astrobiologist Professor Lewis Dartnell to find out just how far the moons influence on life on Earth extends. He also talks to Dr Rohin Francis to dig deeper into the myths surrounding the moons influence on human behaviour, and discuss some of the latest evidence

programme.desc: European and international headlines live via satellite

programme.title: Nightsleeper
programme.desc: With the passengers turning on each other in the aftermath of tragedy, Abby desperately attempts to rally the traumatised Joe and persuade him to take back control. But when Huds shock attempt to stop the train his way puts everyone onboard in more peril, Joe is suddenly left fighting fires on multiple fronts. Real-time thriller, starring Alexandra Roach and Joe Cole

programme.title: Breaking Out
programme.desc: A portrait of Irish musical artist Fergus OFarrell, who was diagnosed with muscular dystrophy at a young age, but didnt allow this define him or limit his musical pursuits. This programme follows Fergus from his teenage days in Dublin to the Czech Republic, to Radio City Music Hall and home to West Cork where he struggles to record his final album

programme.title: Live: Womens Six Nations Rugby
programme.desc: England v Ireland (Kick-off 2.25pm). All the action from both teams opening match of the championship, held at Allianz Stadium Twickenham. England have won this title in each of the last seven years and were also crowned world champions in 2025. They claimed a 49-5 victory when these sides met in Cork in last years competition, though they only led 7-5 at half time. Jacqui Hurley presents, with analysis from Bernard Jackman, Hannah Tyrrell and Lindsay Peat, and commentary by Hugh Cahill and Fiona Coghlan

programme.title: Animal Control

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti visit Wilderness Hotel Inari, an adventure hotel above the Arctic Circle in Lapland. Working in sub-zero conditions, Monica learns about the indigenous Sami culture before prepping reindeer stew, while Rob gets all hot and bothered maintaining saunas before taking a cold-water plunge in a snow hole. But will Rob finally witness the wonder of the Northern Lights from the comfort of his own room?

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Thurso, Scotlands most northerly mainland station, the crew race to the rescue of an ocean-going rower whos run into trouble 40 miles out to sea. In Dungeness, on the Kent coast, there are two different shouts for the crew to deal with - an 11-year-old boy who has drifted out to sea on an inflatable dinghy, and three migrants attempting to cross the English Channel at night

programme.title: Brotherhood of Lions
programme.desc: Documentary following three lionesses and their nine cubs in Kenyas Masai Mara reserve, with cameras focusing on the youngsters journey to adulthood over five years. programme.title: Rush (2013)
programme.desc: The 1976 Formula 1 Championship season is dominated by a fierce rivalry between two contrasting drivers, as daredevil English driver James Hunt and methodical Austrian defending champion Niki Lauda put their lives on the line to outdo each other on the track. Fact-based drama, starring Chris Hemsworth and Daniel Bruhl, with Olivia Wilde and Alexandra Maria Lara

programme.title: The Responder
programme.desc: Chris and Rachel are blackmailed by a top dealer, only for Jodie to offer them a way out. Drama, starring Martin Freeman, Adelayo Adedayo and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: As the drama continues, the traitors second murder victim is revealed at breakfast. The mission leaves the players clutching at straws to find out more about their fellow players. programme.desc: A look at the 1970s, a game-changing decade that brought new sounds and icons like Nancy Wilson and Joan Jett, as well as Debbie Harry of Blondie

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Friends, fans and fellow musicians reflect on Elton Johns career, exploring his greatest hits, as well as his work on The Lion King and Billy Elliot

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: The art collection of the singer-songwriter goes under the hammer at Christies in a special auction featuring works by Tracey Emin, Damien Hirst and Bridget Riley

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: Bonhams auctions a collection of letters written by Jackie Kennedy that she sent to British diplomat David Ormsby-Gore shortly after being widowed

programme.title: Pavarotti in Central Park
programme.desc: The great tenor performs favourite arias, including O Sole Mio and Nessun Dorma, with the New York Philharmonic and the Boys Choir of Harlem

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A devious woman meets her match when she tries to take advantage of a struggling artist

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A wealthy mans wife plans to murder him - but her astute husband proves more ruthless than she bargained for. Starring Telly Savalas and Rita Gam

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An ordinary married couple inherit a fortune that has a dramatic effect on their lives. Starring Frank Finlay and Heather Sears

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Friendships are pushed to their limits when two school chums decide to share a house. Starring Rachael Kempson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A womans gift for her lovers bride-to-be could not be more suitable - or so she thinks

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: The wife of an inveterate gambler tells him to either give up poker or else she will leave him. Starring Gig Young, Martha Hyer and Robert Redford

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: An assailant is brutally attacking women in a small college town and a host of weird staff members are suspects. Starring Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: Spymasters: The Great Spy Writers
programme.sub-title: Alistair MacLean to John le Carre
programme.desc: A look at how Cold War storytelling came alive in books by Fredrick Forsyth, Alistair MacLean and John le Carre, with clips from films including The Third Man

programme.title: The Hollies: Look Through Any Window
programme.desc: Profile of the band, including an interview with members Graham Nash, Allan Clarke, Tony Hicks and Bobby Elliott and archive TV performances by the group from 1963 to 1975

programme.title: Supershow: The Last Great Jam of the 60s
programme.desc: Footage of the 1969 music event, for which musicians such as Eric Clapton and Buddy Guy took over a disused factory in a festival of celebration

programme.title: Bjork: Cornucopia
programme.desc: A 2023 concert by the Icelandic singer-songwriter at the Altice Arena in Lisbon, featuring songs from her albums Utopia, Vulnicura and Fossora

programme.title: The Cure: Live in Hyde Park
programme.desc: A 2018 performance celebrating the veteran indie bands 40th anniversary, featuring songs including Just Like Heaven, In Between Days, A Forest and Boys Dont Cry

programme.title: Siouxsie and the Banshees: Nocturne
programme.desc: A recording of the band performing at the Royal Albert Hall over two nights in 1983, featuring Israel, Cascade, Melt and Pulled to Bits as well as the Beatles Helter Skelter

programme.title: The Public Image Is Rotten
programme.desc: The story of PiL, the groundbreaking band formed by John Lydon after the collapse of Sex Pistols in 1978

programme.title: Whats New Pussycat? (1965)
programme.desc: A soon-to-be-married fashion editor worries hell find it hard to stay faithful and seeks psychiatric help. programme.title: White Riot (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a vital national protest movement - Rock Against Racism, formed in 1976 when a group of artists united to take on the National Front

programme.title: London Calling
programme.desc: How British pop music has been influenced by other art forms, beginning by examining why many bands are formed in the nations art colleges. Featuring contributions by journalists Paul Morley and Simon Reynolds, Blurs Graham Coxon, Lemon Jellys Fred Deakin, Colin Newman of Wire and Heaven 17s Martyn Ware

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Sex Pistols - Never Mind the B*******
programme.desc: The story behind the Sex Pistols sole studio album, 1977s Never Mind the B*******, Heres the Sex Pistols. Featuring interviews with singer John Lydon and manager Malcolm McLaren

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music journalists examine the life and music of Michael Lee Aday, better known as Meat Loaf, the larger-than-life singer-songwriter behind Bat Out of Hell

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics discuss the rise of the veteran rock band whose career spans more than 50 years, from their formation at school in London to sell-out tours around the world

programme.title: Classic FM Live: 25th Anniversary Concert
programme.desc: A musical extravaganza from the Royal Albert Hall, with Mark Wigglesworth conducting Bournemouth Symphony Orchestra and Chorus in Handels Zadok the Priest, Parrys Jerusalem and Puccinis great aria Nessun Dorma. There are also performances from guest artists, including bass-baritone Bryn Terfel, composer John Rutter, violinist Esther Abrami, tenor Pene Pati, Classic FMs composer in residence Debbie Wiseman, and Diana Newell, winner of Channel 4s The Piano.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A casino offers Roger Carson an escape from his domineering aunt and he develops a love for both gambling and a pretty hostess called Suzie. But the casino boss has a score to settle with the Carson family. Andrew Ray, Elaine Paige , Elizabeth Spriggs and Martin Benson star

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A captain has to deal with a man on board his airliner who seizes a stewardess, holds her hostage and demands a million dollars in ransom money and a parachute for himself. Starring Denis Quilley, Suzanne Danielle and Simon Cadell

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A fashion model loses her glamorous lifestyle and is ditched by her boyfriend - but years later she sees an opportunity to win him back

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman eavesdrops on a private conversation while at lunch, which has disastrous repercussions on her marriage

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A retired thief tries to break into a new safe as a publicity stunt, but the money inside proves too much of a temptation for the ex-convict. Starring John Mills and June Ritchie

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A blackmailer threatens to reveal some horrific information to an adopted teenager about her real parents unless her adoptive father pays him off. Starring Brian Keith

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: Five witnesses to a hit-and-run incident at an intersection all say the car did not stop at the stop sign, but are they all wrong? Starring John Forsythe and Evans Evans

programme.title: Julie Andrews Forever
programme.desc: A look at the career of the Oscar-winning actress, who starred in The Sound of Music and Mary Poppins

programme.title: Les Miserables - The Staged Concert (2019)

programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary exploring the 60-year career of the singer-songwriter, looking at his return to the studio to record his 2023 album Seven Psalms

programme.sub-title: Alistair MacLean to John le Carre
programme.desc: A look at how Cold War storytelling came alive in books by Fredrick Forsyth, Alistair MacLean and John le Carre, with clips from films including The Third Man

programme.desc: An Irishman heads to Australia to seek his fortune, where he meets a woman from his past who is now married to a man deported for killing her brother. Alfred Hitchcocks period drama, starring Ingrid Bergman, Michael Wilding, Joseph Cotten and Margaret Leighton

programme.title: Urban Myths

programme.title: Urban Myths
programme.desc: The financially strapped director gets an unlikely opportunity in East Anglia while working on his fiilm F for Fake in 1972. Comedy, starring Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: A spontaneous trip to a Metallica gig causes Axe to question his freedom. Meanwhile, Chuck must assesses his own fathers business dealings. Drama, starring Damian Lewis

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: First episode of the drama set in a lawless US town in the aftermath of Little Big Horn, where everyones a gold digger, from hopeful prospectors to the saloon hookers. Starring Ian McShane as ruthless saloon owner Al Swearengen, Timothy Olyphant as store manager Seth Bullock and Molly Parker as Alma Garrett

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Calamity Jane and Doc Cochran care for the little girl who survived the massacre, as suspicions arise that the Indians may not have been to blame. programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Alma enlists Hickoks help in an attempt to get to the bottom of her husbands death. However, there is also a plot being made against Bills own life

The return of the crime anthology, starring Jodie Foster and Kali Reis

programme.title: The Trip (2010)
programme.desc: Steve Coogan is commissioned to review half a dozen restaurants for a Sunday newspaper and plans the trip with his American girlfriend - only to face the prospect of a week of meals-for-one after she dumps him. So Steve calls old friend Rob Brydon to accompany him, and the pair find themselves debating the big questions of life over a series of delightful dishes. Improvised comedy in which the stars play versions of themselves, directed by Michael Winterbottom

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Lewis and Crosetti expose a woman who has been marrying and then killing men to get her hands on their money. Crime drama, starring Daniel Baldwin

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores the history of Bristol, beginning by learning a grisly secret at the docks before meeting Mercury Prize-winning musician Roni Size to discuss life in the St Pauls and Stokes Croft areas of the city. programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city
