programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D, Nat, John, Linda and the rest of the bidders are in Newcastle-upon-Tyne trying to outbid each other. Not everything is black and white in the north-east city, as savvy bidders gamble on unseen storage units. Feisty new girl Ione clashes with Shaun. Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D, Nat, John, Linda and the rest of the bidders are in Northampton trying to outbid each other. Ione goes head to head with Sam - and who will be left as a bridesmaid when a wedding carriage comes up for grabs? Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Steve Coogan joins the team as Jeremy Clarkson drives the Ferrari 575 M and the Aston Martin Vanquish, both of which feature F1-style paddle-shift gearboxes, and the Lotus Esprit takes the Top Gear power test. Plus, how to create a gadget-packed James Bond car on a tight budget

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The motoring show has a taste of luxury as Jeremy Clarkson road-tests Mercedes Maybach, the largest and most lavish limousine in the world, and celebrates the 30th birthday of the car that has carried dictators, directors and despots, the Mercedes S Class. Plus, the Jaguar XJ and the Bentley Arnage T. Jonathan Ross is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the countrys most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the worlds first carbon neutral country by 2021

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britains famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of takeaway food in Britain and discuss the various ways that it has evolved over the years. The culinary duo also rustle up some of their favourite dishes

programme.title: Robson Greens Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor is joined by his uncle Matheson Green and nephew Daymon Britton, heading to Rothbury in Northumberland to sample a guided bird-watching walk before setting up camp on a farm that rents out converted horseboxes.

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve

Screen hardman Ray Winstone, comic Josh Widdicombe, maths whizz Rachel Riley and actor Ricky Tomlinson join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves. Does one of the contestants really apologise to their boiled egg before smashing its head in? Who apparently writes Christmas cards with their feet? And did one of the line-up really prevent their bandmate joining the Beatles?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Jimmy Carr and Marcus Brigstocke, TV presenter Terry Christian and singer Jamelia join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show, in which they try to dupe their opponents with plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister and Rimmer finally arrive back on Earth, only to discover they have slipped through a time hole into a period where everything occurs backwards. Sci-fi space comedy, starring Chris Barrie and Craig Charles

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The ship is on a collision course with five black holes and Holly recommends that the crew members abandon Red Dwarf and regroup after the danger is over. Cat and Kryten take off in Blue Midget, while Lister and Rimmer make their escape in Starbug, but are marooned when it is hit by a meteor and crash lands on an icy planet. Starring Chris Barrie and Craig Charles

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: A grotesque, salivating genetic mutant goes on the rampage aboard the ship, spreading terror and stealing the crews emotions, leaving Lister without fear and Rimmer with no anger. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles and Frances Barber

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucys discussion about whether they should have another child descends into psychological warfare. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain presents a special compilation show featuring previously unbroadcast material, out-takes and favourite bits from the recent series of the satirical comedy quiz, when regular panellists Andy Parsons and Hugh Dennis were joined by guests including Rob Beckett, Miles Jupp, Romesh Ranganathan, Ed Byrne and Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Gadget Man
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Radio 1 DJ Sara Cox to test innovative devices for helping people get through their morning routines, including a magic mirror and a machine that promises to propel users from the bedroom and wash, dress and feed them automatically. He also encounters some unusual vehicles while experiencing a commute at dawn with John Humphrys, host of Radio 4s Today programme

programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D, Nat, John, Linda and the rest of the bidders are in Newcastle-upon-Tyne trying to outbid each other. Not everything is black and white in the north-east city, as savvy bidders gamble on unseen storage units. Feisty new girl Ione clashes with Shaun. Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Heavy D, Nat, John, Linda and the rest of the bidders are in Northampton trying to outbid each other. Ione goes head to head with Sam - and who will be left as a bridesmaid when a wedding carriage comes up for grabs? Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Steve Coogan joins the team as Jeremy Clarkson drives the Ferrari 575 M and the Aston Martin Vanquish, both of which feature F1-style paddle-shift gearboxes, and the Lotus Esprit takes the Top Gear power test. Plus, how to create a gadget-packed James Bond car on a tight budget

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The motoring show has a taste of luxury as Jeremy Clarkson road-tests Mercedes Maybach, the largest and most lavish limousine in the world, and celebrates the 30th birthday of the car that has carried dictators, directors and despots, the Mercedes S Class. Plus, the Jaguar XJ and the Bentley Arnage T. Jonathan Ross is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the countrys most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the worlds first carbon neutral country by 2021

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britains famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out who ate all the pies on the football pitch

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of takeaway food in Britain and discuss the various ways that it has evolved over the years. The culinary duo also rustle up some of their favourite dishes

programme.desc: The actor is joined by his uncle Matheson Green and nephew Daymon Britton, heading to Rothbury in Northumberland to sample a guided bird-watching walk before setting up camp on a farm that rents out converted horseboxes.

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: A sleep-deprived Moira worries about a stack of old love letters addressed to Johnny from another woman. David and Stevie are the victims of a hold-up at the shop

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Alexis and Johnny hatch a plan to impress an influential travel blogger who might be able to review the motel. David and Patrick search for a new apartment. Eugene Levy stars

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Johnny finds himself under financial pressure, partly due to the arrival of an expensive gown that Moira intends to wear on the red carpet at the premiere of her new movie

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: Patrick is hosting a housewarming party he is planning without Davids help, the latter who expects it to fail because of it. Meanwhile, Jocelyn and Roland ask almost everyone in town but Jonny and Moira if they would be willing to babysit

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty investigates the apparent suicide of Roberts uncle, found hanged at his home, and discovers the whole village where he lived is gripped by superstition. Hetty begins to suspect that the villagers of Readsby - once a notorious haven for witches - are still engaged in satanic practices, and is determined to prove that they were somehow involved

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Snooker player Dennis Taylor asks Lovejoy for help finding a billiards table that once belonged to Mary Queen of Scots. Drama, also starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A flag from the American War of Independence turns up in a local church and triggers a wave of public interest as a number of villagers come forward to claim it as their own. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: New Tricks

Brian is puzzled by Esthers odd behaviour and her new-found interest in the internet, leading him to think the unthinkable - she must be having an affair. Paul McGann and Jesse Birdsall guest star, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Jonathan Creek
programme.desc: A former sex kitten tells a tabloid newspaper all about her promiscuous past, much to the disgust of villagers, and later suffers a tragic accident that leaves her in a coma. Shortly after, she is spotted making her way to church, remorseful and ready to seek forgiveness for her sins. Jonathan and Maddy are baffled but not convinced that her recovery has a supernatural explanation. Emma Kennedy guest stars

programme.title: Signora Volpe
programme.desc: A British spy-turned-detective moves to Italy, but trouble follows her. Sylvia Fox (Emilia Fox) is at a wedding when the groom disappears and a womans body is found

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When the Brokenwood Small Business Association stage a Mexican Day of the Dead night market, they dont bargain on an actual dead body becoming part of the festivities

programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: Part one of two. Miles and Chandlers latest case takes them deep into the world of modern art after the discovery of a flayed human face at Whitechapel Gallery. Interpreting some Russian prison tattoos suggests a link to organised crime, but what is the significance of the mysterious footsteps heard in empty corridors at the police station? As the investigation progresses, the killer strikes again, leaving a coded message, and Buchans desperate search for answers leads him on a dangerous path. Starring Phil Davis, Rupert Penry-Jones, and Steve Pemberton, who also wrote this episode

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley faces charges of intimidating a suspect, and is suspended from duty, meaning Havers and new partner DI Fiona Knight (guest star Liza Tarbuck) must investigate when the body of a woman is found in a lake. However, the detective involves himself in the case, compromising his chances of an unbiased trial. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic, fearing they were being abused or neglected. Her husband admits that she has been behaving strangely lately, and when she is referred to a neurologist by Dr Turner, the truth proves devastating for the whole family. Christopher admits that Alexandra has been unhappy since his divorce, and Violet and Fred organise a beauty contest to raise money for charity. Valerie and Magda both sign up and quickly become close friends - leading to the sharing of a terrible secret

programme.desc: Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a second wife into their home, who is already eight months pregnant. Trixie hits the bottle following her break-up with Christopher, Dr Turner tries to persuade Sister Monica Joan to admit that her eyesight is failing, and Lucille helps a woman with a compulsion to eat coal

programme.desc: First in a two-part drama, starring Billie Whitelaw as a herbalist who takes an 11-year-old orphan under her wing. The youngster soon makes friends with a local girl and the future looks rosy - but heartbreak is waiting just around the corner. With Tim Healy, Jonathan Kerrigan and Michael Jayston

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called by DI Gillespie to the home of minister of defence Charles Streatham, where guest Mary Sparkes has been found dead at the bottom of a staircase. Quickly assessing Sparkes has been murdered by poisoning rather than by a fall, Gillespie and DS Livingstone have to identify the murderer among the persons in the house - one is reporter Ruth Penny, disguised as a maid and looking for a scoop

programme.title: Campion

programme.desc: Part two of two. After learning the name of their leader, Campion thwarts the international gang attempting to steal the Gyrth Chalice with some local help. Peter Davison and Brian Glover star in the mystery drama series based on Margery Allinghams stories

programme.desc: Harry and Fergus travel to the convent of a silent order of nuns when a friend asks Harry to investigate a suspicious suicide, believing foul play is afoot

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The Lyell team investigates the murder of a family shot while picnicking by a river, and discover the female victim was in the middle of a bitter divorce. Local detective Malcolm Guillam is brought in to help and when the present-day murder reminds him of an unsolved case that haunts him, he sees an opportunity to redeem himself. However, DCI Sally Butcher is convinced that the womans estranged husband is responsible for the murder and has no time for Guillams trip down memory lane. Feature-length episode, guest starring Derek Griffiths

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a bizarre block in Taiwan resembling a giant speaker, a pioneering venture in Alaska, and an alpine village that masks a military beast

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the wild and rugged coastline of Northumberland by rail, beginning in Morpeth, where he finds an historic collection of Northumbrian bagpipes and discovers how difficult they are to play. The East Coast mainline delivers Michael to Widdrington and its long sandy bay, where a beachcombing blacksmith finds treasures to inspire artwork in steel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Farne Islands to Berwick upon Tweed
programme.desc: Michael Portillos rail trek along the coast of Northumberland continues towards the Farne Islands, takes in the holy island of Lindisfarne, and finishes in Englands northernmost town, Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris in the Outer Hebrides, beginning with a visit to a nuclear power station on the Hunterston Peninsula. He then learns about Largs Viking past and admires one of the most beautiful railway stations in Britain at Wemyss Bay. The importance of ferries to connect western Scotland with its many islands becomes plain to Michael at Port Glasgow in the deep waters of the Clyde, where he visits Ferguson Marine, builders of Calmac ferries

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotlands west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago from the ferry terminal of Oban. On his way to the Isle of Mull, Michael watches eagerly for sea mammals in a stretch of water where many species of whales, dolphins and porpoises have been recorded, before checking out the daily catch in the fishing port of Tobermory. Later, Michael heads to the tiny Isle of Staffa, where he follows in the wake of a young Felix Mendelssohn, who was only 20-year-old when he set eyes on the volcanic sea cavern, Fingals Cave

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.sub-title: Londons Lost Route to the Sea
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go in search of the lost route between London and the sea, which has not been fully open for more than 200 years and requires four different boats to traverse now. They leave the Thames at Teddington and travel through the Surrey and Sussex countryside before arriving at Guildford, and a canal-side theatre where they have both regularly performed. The final leg of the trip brings them to the River Arun before reaching the English Channel

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel from Birmingham to Braunston, Northamptonshire, to attend a festival of vintage canal boats, aided on the tricky journey by their son Sam and Juliet, Timothys daughter from his first marriage. Along the way, they meet a 78-year-old boatman, who reveals how he was born on a canal boat, and unveil a plaque dedicated to a woman who dedicated her life to preserving Britains canals

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales go to Ireland and travel along the Shannon Erne Waterway, a canal linking Northern Ireland with the Republic of Ireland. Along the way, they try some traditional dancing and folk singing, visit historic sites including Crom Castle and Bloody Pass, and meet a genealogist with a fascinating story to tell

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales set forth from Gothenburg on the first half of the Gota Canal, one of the longest and most beautiful waterways in the world

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the worlds carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of omnivores, natures generalists, revealing their myriad talents in the wild and how some species have adapted to mans encroachment into their habitats by making their homes in cities and towns

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough swims alongside sea otters, discovering he is no match for their natural agility in the water, while in Florida he takes a dip with manatees for the first time and observes their feeding habits. On the coast of California, he encounters the largest mammals on the planet, blue whales, and reveals how they, along with porpoises and dolphins, learned to court, mate and give birth in the water

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough profiles the tree-dwellers of the animal kingdom, as primates, bats and squirrels perform complicated manoeuvres in the worlds forests. Whether aided by sharp claws, webbed feet or gripping tails, these animals play a complicated game of survival with predators lurking below, ready to make a meal of those who fail to stay aloft

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: We Have Put Her Living in the Tomb
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret agree to pet-sit a tortoise for a friend who ought to know better, and an anniversary outing to the theatre ends in disaster. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household echoes to the sound of laughter, as Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and Victor begins penning a sitcom script. However, his mother-in-laws bath-time accident and a run-in with British Telecom soon leave the curmudgeonly codger in no mood for jokes. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: A persistent door-to-door salesman makes grouchy Victor Meldrew see red - and an unending stream of junk mail through his letterbox threatens to push him over the brink into insanity. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Angus Deayton star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret set off for a break by the seaside - but the discovery of a dead gull in their boarding-house bathroom sets the scene for what turns out to be a weekend of sheer hell. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star, with a guest appearance by Stephen Lewis of On the Buses fame

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor endures a sleepless night - and naturally ensures the eternally put-upon Margaret and their neighbours share in his suffering. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through some of the most remote landscapes on Earth. He begins in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, encountering thick jungles and tropical swamps as he looks for the people and wildlife whose home is Africas greatest rainforest. His first destination is the remote village of Baka, where Simon experiences a culture based on sharing resources and respecting all living things in the rainforest

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: In Luton Airport No One Can Hear You Scream
programme.desc: The Meldrews return from an overseas holiday to find yet another disaster awaiting them - their home has been demolished. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.desc: The Ford Focus is put through its paces, the Ferrari Enzo burns rubber on the track, and the supercars of the 1980s are pitted against their modern-day counterparts. programme.desc: David Attenborough profiles the tree-dwellers of the animal kingdom, as primates, bats and squirrels perform complicated manoeuvres in the worlds forests. Whether aided by sharp claws, webbed feet or gripping tails, these animals play a complicated game of survival with predators lurking below, ready to make a meal of those who fail to stay aloft

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy becomes a yuppie, complete with red braces, Filofax and chrome briefcase. Meanwhile, Rodney begins attending a computer course at night school, where he meets Cassandra. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy has her god-daughter dumped on her overnight, which wouldnt be so bad except the girls stay coincides with her birthday - leaving the inexperienced guardian and her flatmate Lee struggling to come up with appropriate entertainment for a bunch of excited nine-year-olds. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star, with a guest appearance by Paul Kaye (Game of Thrones)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot and Gary get serious over a chess contest in which the victor gains an evening of pleasure. Laddish comedy, starring Martin Clunes and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: A hypnotist is booked to provide the entertainment at the triplets baptism - and Agnes soon finds herself under his spell. Meanwhile, the matriarchs kitchen is falling apart - but that turns out to be the least of her problems when son Mark delivers some heartbreaking news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggys penfriend comes to stay, but some of the staff are not convinced his intentions are entirely honourable. Comedy, starring Su Pollard

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin organises a fundraising dance, and suspects that Paul will sabotage his arrangements. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Peter Egan and Penelope Wilton. First broadcast February 26, 1984

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie faces his worst nightmare when Marlenes sister decides to pay a visit from Peckham. As if the man-eating glamourpuss was not enough to cope with, he finds all his staff tied up in the barn - and realises his past has come back to haunt him. Guest starring Paula Wilcox

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney mourn Grandads death, and feel gratified to see such a good turn-out of friends and relatives at his funeral - even if most people are more interested in the food afterwards. programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del snatches a chance to make a quick profit, leaving gullible Denzil stripped of his redundancy money. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dodgy dealer Del gets his hands on a consignment of self-inflating rubber dolls, which turn out to be filled with explosive gas. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A retired jeweller offers Del-Boy the deal of a lifetime - then suddenly has a heart attack and gets taken to hospital with a briefcase containing 250 cut-price gold necklaces still chained to his wrist. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del enters one of Rodneys old paintings in a competition and is over the moon when he wins a holiday for three at a five-star hotel in Majorca. Soon after, the Trotter brothers and Cassandra arrive at Palma Airport - but Del-Boy reveals there is a slight snag. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Dels beloved mum apparently makes contact during a seance at the Nags Head, inspiring him to visit the doctor to seek treatment for stomach cramps. Admitted to hospital for tests, he fears there is something seriously wrong with him - but a bacon sandwich and an alcoholic drink smuggled in by Rodney soon put the smile back on his face. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney ties the knot with Cassandra, but Del has more on his mind than best man duties as the dreaded Driscoll brothers arrive at the Nags Head baying for his blood over some stolen mobile phones. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Gwyneth Strong

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: Richie and Eddies nocturnal peace is disturbed by a violent encounter with a burglar - resulting in the intruders sack of loot falling into their sticky hands. Paul Bradley guests, with Adrian Edmondson and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Bottom
programme.desc: After inheriting £600 the duo head off for a night at the fair. Richies wallet is stolen, but that proves to be the least of his problems as a fortune teller declares that he is going to die in three nights time. Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney aspires to be a pop star, taking on the role of the drumless drummer in Mental Mickeys band but, not surprisingly, Del-Boy manages to ruin his chances. With a guest appearance by DJ Mike Read, alongside David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. However, things soon take a turn for the better when Dels father-in-law finds an old watch among the Trotters junk, which he claims could net them a pretty packet at auction. But nothing can quite prepare them for the shock they have in store. The final part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del feels down on his luck after the council rejects his application for a home improvement grant, but his spirits are raised when he hears about a fancy-dress party across town. David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield star as the wheeler-dealers in the first part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy. With Tessa Peake-Jones, Gwyneth Strong, Douglas Hodge and Angela Bruce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: With the Walmington-on-Sea Home Guard now established, Captain Mainwaring sets about trying to get equipment for the platoon so they can see off potential Nazi invaders. Classic comedy first shown in 1968, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaigns advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isnt far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Tim Vine. Tim arrives home after a night out clubbing and realises he has picked up the wrong coat, one which contains a pocket full of drugs. After discovering the owner is Larry the Butcher Stubbs, he enlists Lees help to return the jacket without ending up in serious danger

programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaigns advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isnt far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Raquel invites her estranged parents around for dinner, and Del-Boy is keen to make an impression - but a mix-up with the gravy boat puts a damper on proceedings. However, things soon take a turn for the better when Dels father-in-law finds an old watch among the Trotters junk, which he claims could net them a pretty packet at auction. But nothing can quite prepare them for the shock they have in store. The final part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Tim puts the flat on the market in the wake of Kates departure, forcing Lee to come up with a plan to allow him to stay put. However, moving the landlords sister Lucy in proves far from ideal when the pair end up in a bidding war. Comedy, starring Lee Mack

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot offers his body and Gary fixes up a burglar alarm in forlorn efforts to impress Deborah

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy works on a government health initiative encouraging men to regularly check themselves for lumps, so an eager Lee follows the campaigns advice - only to discover something unexpected. Comedy, starring Sally Bretton, Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Katy Wix

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and Daisy go on a skiing trip to Eastern Europe, but as usual disaster isnt far behind. The trio of friends find themselves stuck in a cable car with a handful of other nervous tourists - one of whom just happens to be pregnant. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Hancock: Very Nearly an Armful
programme.desc: Jack Dee hosts a unique look back at the life and career of one of Britains most important and influential comedy stars, Tony Hancock

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: More comedy sketches, including Nan visiting a pound shop, and Lauren discovering she has not been invited to a party

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.sub-title: The Nine Lives of Teddy Hart
programme.desc: Teddy meets aspiring wrestler Samantha Fiddler and takes her to Florida to train, but when he returns to Canada, Samanthas family reports her missing

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Max sets out to find the teenage murder suspect who has gone on the run after being tipped off about the police sting by his stepmother. Stuart voices his concern that his colleague is letting personal involvement with the family cloud his judgement - an opinion borne out when Max confronts the armed youngster without telling anybody of his plans. Honeysuckle Weeks guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max, Grace and Jacob arrest a drug dealer as he makes a deal but his runner escapes, his face concealed by a hoodie. The investigation takes a serious turn when the youngster crashes the stolen car he is driving and runs off, leaving a passenger fighting for her life. The unidentified fugitive is eventually traced to a bail hostel, where three possible suspects are taken in for questioning, but a false confession makes the detectives job doubly difficult

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The resident magician of a social club turns to crime to fund a gambling habit and falls prey to a blackmailer threatening to harm his girlfriend. Neil and Stevie persuade the hapless entertainer to take part in a sting operation at the club but the suspect under surveillance becomes involved in a brawl and the money vanishes into thin air

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Beth shocks Tony by revealing she has applied for a transfer, but before they can discuss it further they spot a woman preparing to jump to her death from a bridge over the River Thames. Nate eventually rescues her from the water, only to find she is carrying a bag of heroin. Investigations into the source of the drugs focus on the boxing club where she works

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Beth prepares for her last day at Sun Hill, her colleagues urge her to show her feelings for Nate at her leaving party. She is pleased to find herself paired up with him on a call about a domestic disturbance in which a woman is found unconscious at the bottom of the stairs. The officers are surprised to discover their prime suspect is the boxing club owner they interviewed the previous day about drug dealing on his premises

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A lorry driver left for dead by masked raiders who hijacked his vehicle in an isolated lay-by admits to smuggling a woman and her child into the country to help them escape a violent marriage. The search is on to locate the stolen vehicle but when it is finally traced and thoroughly searched, Mel suspects her colleague Arun has tipped off the fugitives, abusing his position as a police officer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Leon and Callum investigate the return of stolen goods to the house from which they were taken - apparently by a remorseful burglar. They discover their suspect is covering for his drug addict sister, who cannot escape the clutches of her violent dealer, but in the ensuing sting operation £300 goes missing and suspicion falls on Leon himself

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two YouTube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Paramedic Kim climbs into a crushed car balanced over a fast-flowing river, while services across the Pennines are paralysed when a man plunges from a bridge onto a busy rail line

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Join ambulances, helicopters and boats across New Zealand, transporting critical patients to safety. Paramedics find a motocross rider near death in a remote forest

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A man is at risk of losing his leg after a horrific accident with a boat propeller, while another needs to be airlifted to hospital after an accident involving a tractor

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The Shipmans drive to Wales for the wedding fayre, and after a momentous trip to the church for the reading of the banns, Nessa tells Stacey her big secret. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The bride-to-be goes on a hen party, while Gavin embarks on two stag nights, the second of which reveals shocking secrets about his fiancee that could jeopardise the wedding. Elsewhere, the early return of Staceys brother Jason horrifies Uncle Bryn. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The day of the wedding arrives and Bryn is still behaving oddly around Staceys brother. Nessa debates whether to share her big secret and Smithy resolves to give a superb best mans speech - but finds himself overcome with emotion under the spotlight. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.desc: With Samanthas family desperate for news, Teddy answers Fredericks questions. As new information comes to light, one of Teddys ex-students speaks up

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: A handsome stranger comes to the rescue when Susan is stranded at an Albanian airport, and the dashing American invites her to join him on his round-the-world adventure. Ben is surprised when a celebrity dentist dedicates a new book to him, and finds out the author is an old friend from college who is actually jealous of the family mans mundane lifestyle. Guest starring John Barrowman and David Haig

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben goes undercover in a hotel when he suspects Susan of having an affair with a wealthy client, but finds Janey there instead. After recovering from the shock, he begins imagining the benefits of a rich son-in-law - until Susan sees him kissing another woman. Guest starring Robert Bathurst

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan offers her support to an old friend going through a divorce, and Ben is roped in as a date to make the womans husband jealous. However, a large amount of celebratory wine leads to a tricky situation. Elsewhere, Janey invents a Swedish woman to be Rogers love interest. Guest starring Belinda Lang (2point4 Children)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed by the inheritance he receives when his uncle dies, while another investment causes problems for the dentist. Robert Lindsay stars

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When Ben and Susan discover Michael has been seeing a therapist, but will not tell his parents why, they are prompted to undertake a spot of self-analysis

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan wants a dog, but the assessor from the dogs home deems her unsuitable, so Ben goes to the kennels to sneak one out for her, only to find she is already there and about to call in the press as a protest against the assessors decision

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats a seven-month-old fox red Labrador puppy with lameness, caused by a joint defect called osteochondritis dissecans. He explains that medicine can be given, but in the end the dog may end up requiring a complete ankle replacement or a joint fusion on both sides. Jo has travelled 400 miles to bring her beloved four-year-old springer spaniel to see Noel. The poochs kneecap keeps slipping in and out of her knee joint, and despite several previous procedures, the problems persist. After an examination by Noel, Jo faces the further worry of osteoarthritis

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Spaniel Scrumpy is suffering from lameness and Noel discovers he has a collapsed disc and severe elbow arthritis. He already has a chequered medical history totalling six previous operations to his elbows, hip and knee. Meanwhile, there is an emergency admission when a 15-month-old dachshund arrives with a mystery brain condition, and Noel and his neurology colleagues have their work cut out to identify the cause. When his condition rapidly deteriorates overnight, suddenly the life of this much-loved young dog hangs in the balance

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Four-year-old pug and beagle cross Barney is brought into Fitzpatrick Referrals after being hit by a bus. The dog has lost 40 per cent of the bone in the damaged leg, so Noel creates an innovative two-tier treatment, involving an external ankle frame for support. Sisters Rosemary and Diane bring in their five-year-old basset hound Albert when they find he is suddenly unable to move his back legs, and 10-month-old Labrador cross Gylly receives treatment for lameness and an unusual bone deformity

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Golden retriever poodle-cross Sukis front legs have developed abnormally and now require urgent attention as the bones of her forearms are growing at different rates, so Noel decides that major surgery is required. Beloved elderly Labrador Raffa is nearly 11 and has become lame with constant pain in his front legs, and Noel discovers cartilage erosion in both elbows. Meanwhile, five-year-old dachshund Zizi is rushed in after suddenly losing the use of her back legs overnight, and an urgent MRI scan confirms that Zizi has suffered an exploded disc

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics in Oxford treat a woman suffering from a mystery illness, while in Portsmouth, the crews treat an elderly diabetic patient with dangerously low blood sugar

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews attend a man who has fallen off his bike and a four-year-old struggling with tonsillitis. Plus, a 35-year-old man with chest pains proves very hard to find

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: The team tackles a dramatic operation where a patients heart is stopped and restarted. Zoe has a stent replaced in her ureter, which was damaged by her treatment for cancer

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Trisha has an investigation of her womb to find out if she has cancer, Steves varicose veins are stripped from his leg, and Dennis needs an operation to strengthen his eyelid

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A take-charge social media influencer clashes with a sexist biker when she swaps lives with his subservient homemaker wife who prefers regressive gender roles

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A gothic witch and her warlock husband swap lives with a Christian family whose deliverance ministry specializes in casting out demons and evil spirits

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics in Oxford treat a woman suffering from a mystery illness, while in Portsmouth, the crews treat an elderly diabetic patient with dangerously low blood sugar

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews attend a man who has fallen off his bike and a four-year-old struggling with tonsillitis. Plus, a 35-year-old man with chest pains proves very hard to find

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: The team tackles a dramatic operation where a patients heart is stopped and restarted. Zoe has a stent replaced in her ureter, which was damaged by her treatment for cancer

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Trisha has an investigation of her womb to find out if she has cancer, Steves varicose veins are stripped from his leg, and Dennis needs an operation to strengthen his eyelid

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketches, featuring Paul and Sam, the foul-mouthed Nan and Bernie the incompetent nurse

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: More comedy sketches, including Nan visiting a pound shop, and Lauren discovering she has not been invited to a party

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: A death row wife finds love with a convicted murderer, and new parents are determined not to let their baby run their lives. Peter Kay guest stars

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didnt give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called Wagatha Christie, which followed accuisations between footballers wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a 49-year-old who has fallen in the street after a night out in Stoke, while in Dudley, paramedics visit a five-month-old who has a temperature

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: American Historys Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley debunks the myths behind the American Civil War, revealing how its history has been retold over the years to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division. She reveals how Abraham Lincolns personal views, and the behaviour of his troops towards African Americans, were not as noble as they appeared. She also learns how history was rewritten in a bid to downplay the evils of slavery, and how a 1905 blockbuster film about the Civil War relaunched the Ku Klux Klan with terrifying results

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Scottish railway journey reaches Aberdeen. From the granite city, Michael heads out to Forvie National Nature Reserve, which is 1,000 hectares of protected coastal habitat dominated by grassy dunes

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the Culloden Viaduct aboard the Caledonian Sleeper to arrive bright and early in Inverness, from where he heads north on a magnificent scenic adventure to the Orkney archipelago. On boggy moorland overlooking the Moray Firth near Inverness, Michael explores the battlefield at Culloden, where Bonnie Prince Charlies Jacobite army fought British Government troops in 1746

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train north from Inverness to the Orkney Islands. Beginning on the Moray Firth, Michael admires the Cullen Viaduct then enjoys a bowl of Cullen skink at a family-run fishmonger of 150 years standing. In the factory smokery, he learns from the owner how the famous soup acquires its distinctive taste

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Scottish coastal railway journey reaches Invergordon on the Cromarty Firth, the third great inlet on the northeast coast of the Highlands. He arrives on Britains northernmost railway, the Far North Line, and heads straight onto the water to explore a former oil drilling rig now moored in the firth

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis clears a barn full of old treasures. His buys include a weathervane and the wooden sides of a horse-drawn cart, which undergo an amazing reinvention

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary goes hell-for-leather organising an antiques fair. He buys some military tripods and a vintage truck grille and repurposes them as stunning pieces of furniture

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Client Mark has always dreamed of owning a chrome-bumpered MGB convertible, but fixers Sheldon Nichols and Peter Harriott must find clever ways to finance that dream

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Driving instructor Brian loved his Fiat 500 until it rusted away - now Sheldon and Peter must come up with a dream car for no more than £10,000 to make any money

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates face a day of hard labour digging drains on a lonely stretch of moorland. However, Fletcher soon finds a way of turning the situation to his advantage. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming explores the Victorian vWest Yorkshire village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. He also moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom finishes the summer with new refurbished windows before he and Sam head to St Agnes to buy Cornish tin to cast a workshop sign

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: In the third part of his journey, Simon travels through the Andes mountains from Peru to Bolivia, beginning at the world-famous ruins of Machu Pichu. He then visits a remote valley where the mostly indigenous population farms coca leaves. the primary ingredient of cocaine. Heading across the border into Bolivia, Simon discovers how the indigenous women known as cholas have battled for increasing influence and representation

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: On the fourth leg of his journey through the continent, Simon travels through three of the worlds most extreme environments as he takes in the salt flats of Bolivia, Brazils Pantanal wetland region and Paraguays Chaco Forest. In Bolivia, he meets a family making a living carving salt from the vast white expanse of the Uyuni flats, while in Brazil he has a close encounter with South Americas apex predator, the jaguar. In Paraguay, Simon visits a Mennonites school, where traditionally clothed children are drilled in Bible texts and there are no smartphones to be seen

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor operates one of the worlds biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Charlie picks up an iconic 60s motor - a Ford Cortina Deluxe - and a 1970s Morris van brings a taste of Carry On Camping to Pickering

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move an iconic Deltic diesel, better known as The Royal Highland Fusilier, halfway across the country to the Telford Steam Fair

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave Mathewson gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he cant remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The latest stage of Michael Portillos trek takes him past lochs and castles on the Far North Line to Britains northernmost railway station at Thurso. From Lairg on the banks of Loch Shin, Michael heads for a secret conservation area in the 18-mile-long loch to track down one of Scotlands most elusive and endangered species, the freshwater pearl mussel

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Britains northernmost station at Thurso in Caithness, Michael Portillo heads for the ferry port of Scrabster to cross the Pentland Firth to the Orkney Islands. Passing the Old Man of Hoy, Michael takes in stunning views of the Orkney archipelago and the rugged coastlines of its 70 islands

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Tynemouth on the first leg of a trip to Berwick-upon-Tweed, hearing about of a 19th-century tragedy at sea that inspired 140 local men to set up a coastal rescue service. At Long Sands beach, Michael rolls up his sleeves to help volunteers caring for sick and injured seals at the Tynemouth Aquarium Seal Hospital

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Newcastle to Lynemouth, and is intrigued to hear of its part in Newcastles early industrial development. En route, Michael travels through the huge Northumberland coalfield and, at Cramlington, he alights to visit an extraordinary work of landform art made from the spoil of a coalmine, the vast Northumberlandia, known as the Lady of the North

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchills forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, Americas reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitlers decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing Americas decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitlers declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out on the last leg of his journey across central England. While crossing an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon, the narrowboat enthusiast gets stuck in a lock in the heart of Birmingham. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a 170-mile journey across the north of England. However, he soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming explores the Victorian vWest Yorkshire village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. He also moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: An investigation into how recent archaeological finds on the archipelago indicate that Orkney was the cultural capital of Stone Age Britain and the birthplace of the stone circle cult. Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkneys unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkneys unusual geology

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britains poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the regions farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the countys iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across the North and the Midlands, Robbie faces issues with ice on the Grand Union Canal. He takes on Foxtons famous lock flight in Leicestershire and completes his journey in the picturesque village Braunston, Northamptonshire, regarded as the spiritual home for narrowboaters

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming journeys through Royal Leamington Spa and the historic town of Warwick with its medieval castle.

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom finishes the summer with new refurbished windows before he and Sam head to St Agnes to buy Cornish tin to cast a workshop sign

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob takes Terry on a tour of their old haunts - only to reveal most of them have been demolished. Terry finds it difficult accepting that times have changed during his time away - and a job interview proves the last straw. Classic comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry decides he would like to track down some of his former girlfriends - little suspecting the shocks that await him. James Bolam stars

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma throw a surprise party to cheer up Terry - but the blindfolded guest of honour begins to insult old friends, unaware they are listening to his every word

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics

Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkneys unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkneys unusual geology

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britains poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the regions farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the countys iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across the North and the Midlands, Robbie faces issues with ice on the Grand Union Canal. He takes on Foxtons famous lock flight in Leicestershire and completes his journey in the picturesque village Braunston, Northamptonshire, regarded as the spiritual home for narrowboaters

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming navigates the River Severn, and takes an unexpected bath as he tumbles at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming journeys through Royal Leamington Spa and the historic town of Warwick with its medieval castle.

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom finishes the summer with new refurbished windows before he and Sam head to St Agnes to buy Cornish tin to cast a workshop sign

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob takes Terry on a tour of their old haunts - only to reveal most of them have been demolished. Terry finds it difficult accepting that times have changed during his time away - and a job interview proves the last straw. Classic comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
