The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at the impact of summer in the northern hemisphere and winter in the south, revealing that wildlife faces feast, famine, fire and torrential rain in different regions around the world. On Marion Island life has frozen to a standstill, with king penguin chicks facing starvation as their parents left months ago, while in northern Australia its the dry season, and hooded parrots raise their chicks in termite mounds

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian and chat-show host presents a new selection of shaggy dog stories as he gets to know the canine residents of Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London. Paul meets a Dalmatian cross who has been abandoned - not long before giving birth. He also helps a hugely overweight shar-pei cross get back into shape and has his heartstrings pulled by a Yorkshire terrier which was found roaming Battersea Park

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian meets more of the canine residents of Battersea Dogs and Cats Home in London, including a German shepherd puppy that was rejected by its mother at birth and a frisky Jack Russell that is having trouble using one of its back legs. Paul also comes to the aid of a shell-shocked old-timer that has been dumped outside the home and now needs help regaining a little confidence

programme.title: The Royal
programme.sub-title: All the Fun of the Fair
programme.desc: The residents are pleased when a fair comes to Elsinby, but Jeffrey and Samantha have their work cut out when the waltzer operator becomes trapped in the rides mechanism. Hes rushed to the hospital, where Mr Rose operates, and reveals his father has been suffering dizzy spells recently. A concerned Jeffrey heads back to the fairground, but hes too late to prevent disaster. Meanwhile, Ken hatches a dubious money-making scheme and Jill is forced to confront a patients wife. Paul Fox, Anna Madeley and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A group of drunken mods arrive at the hospital in a shocking state, and Ken Hopkirk soon makes the grim discovery that they have been drinking industrial alcohol supplied by their friend, theatre technician Alun Morris. Meanwhile, a fourth member of the boozing group is brought in and has his stomach pumped immediately - but it soon emerges that the illicit hooch has blinded him. Elsewhere, Mr Rose and his surgical team treat a widow with a gangrenous toe at her farmhouse, and student nurse Samantha Beaumont receives a telling-off for laying out operating instruments in an incorrect order. Andy Wear, Michael Starke and Robert Daws star, with a guest appearance by Thelma Barlow

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at autumn in the northern hemisphere and spring in the south, seasons of change that bring opportunities, but also huge challenges. By September temperatures in northern Canada can plummet as low as -20C, but there is one stretch of river that still flows and bears are able to fish for the final salmon of the season. In southern Africa the rains have been gone for many months and watering holes have all-but dried up, while a king penguin chick on Marion Island answers the call of the ocean

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul helps out with dogs who have a variety of issues, including a pair of old labradors that cannot bear to be apart for one second, a Staffy that hates being left alone and a tiny puppy with an anti-social behaviour problem

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital staff have an intriguing case on their hands when local tramp Sebastian Fox is found dead. Ken Hopkirk rummages through his belongings and is surprised to find war medals, leading to the discovery that he was a decorated Navy hero. When a relative is traced but refuses to help with funeral arrangements, the Royal team decide to give him a fitting send-off by scattering his ashes at sea. Meanwhile, Jeffrey Goodwin is shocked to discover a farmers cattle herd have contracted a disease which has passed into cheese thats available in the area, and Jill Weatherill, Matron and Sister Brigid encounter a runaway girl. Rural medical drama, starring Michael Starke and Wendy Craig

programme.title: The Royal

Romance blossoms for Dr Goodwin and student nurse Samantha Beaumont, and Lizzie Kennoways driving lesson ends in disaster when she slams into the back of Mr Harpers parked car. programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: An emergency call comes in to the stroke unit at Bronglais Hospital, Aberystwyth. Issie is becoming more confident in helping stroke patients when they arrive, but is quickly reminded she has a lot to learn and experience to gain. A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick meets Kathleen Chada, whose husband murdered her young sons Eoghan and Ruairi in 2013

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Adrian Dunbar, Jo Brand, Layton Williams and their relatives compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: My Hoarder Mum and Me
programme.desc: A Place in the Sun presenter Jasmine Harman embarks on a personal journey to help her mother Vasoulla overcome her extreme hoarding habit. Over the course of 30 years, the familys five-bedroom London home has become filled from floor to ceiling with clutter, forcing Jasmines 12-year-old brother Cameron to move out. Debate still rages over whether hoarding should be classified as a mental illness, and Jasmine meets the families of other sufferers to learn how they deal with the problem. Narrated by Rupert Houseman

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlins Tempelhof Airport and Londons Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a lady who wants to be able to smile for the first time in 30 years, and a music teacher who would love to become a published author. Plus, a schoolboy with dreams of becoming a professional footballer, and a young father desperate to thank the kind stranger who saved his life

Emotions run high as time ticks away without the correct paperwork, so its down to Passenger Experience Manager Harry to come to the rescue - but is it already too late?

programme.desc: Documentary following three people with Tourettes syndrome as they reach turning points in their lives at a camp that could change everything for them and their families

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: Out There
programme.desc: A man finds himself fighting back against the dark urban menace making their way into his community. Nathan is forced to do a deal with the devil to protect his family, his land and his livelihood. Drama set in a rural community, starring Martin Clunes and Mark Lewis Jones

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: Body in the Bin: The Murder of Louise Kam
programme.desc: What began as an audacious plot to defraud £5million from unsuspecting 71-year-old mother and businesswoman Louise Kam ended in her murder, after which her body was callously dumped in a wheelie bin on a driveway belonging to one of the killers. This documentary follows this shocking story of fraud, murder and the impact of social media

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: The 2002 murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, detailing events from the desperate 14-day police search to the discovery of their bodies

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Shanghai Tower, which at a height of 632 metres is the second tallest building in the world, With a weight of 850,000 tons, it has been built to withstand winds of typhoon proportions

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a young couple desperate to start a family against all odds and a brave father hoping to give his young daughter the gift of life for the second time. She also finds out about a single mum looking for love, an IT manager pledging to take to the stage and a lady who wants nothing more than to be able to hear her childrens voices

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Managers at the Rio Park have clocked off for a bank holiday so its a great opportunity for junior staff to prove themselves. Abi is in charge on reception, looking after new team member Raquel and dealing with a tech malfunction, while inclement weather leaves lifeguard Daniel with nothing to do until lovebirds Andy and Mandy turn up for a swimming lesson

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Live Racing
programme.sub-title: Horse Racing 2026 Live: Aintree - Grand National Festival - Day 3
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 4.00 Grand National from Aintree, the most prestigious race on the British calendar, plus support races at 1.20, 1.55, 2.30 and 3.05. Nick Rockett was ridden to victory by Patrick Mullins last year at a price of 33/1. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team.

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: An emergency call comes in to the stroke unit at Bronglais Hospital, Aberystwyth. Issie is becoming more confident in helping stroke patients when they arrive, but is quickly reminded she has a lot to learn and experience to gain. A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: My Hoarder Mum and Me
programme.desc: A Place in the Sun presenter Jasmine Harman embarks on a personal journey to help her mother Vasoulla overcome her extreme hoarding habit. Over the course of 30 years, the familys five-bedroom London home has become filled from floor to ceiling with clutter, forcing Jasmines 12-year-old brother Cameron to move out. Debate still rages over whether hoarding should be classified as a mental illness, and Jasmine meets the families of other sufferers to learn how they deal with the problem. Narrated by Rupert Houseman

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlins Tempelhof Airport and Londons Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a lady who wants to be able to smile for the first time in 30 years, and a music teacher who would love to become a published author. Plus, a schoolboy with dreams of becoming a professional footballer, and a young father desperate to thank the kind stranger who saved his life

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff are gearing up for a weekend of fun and screams as Halloween arrives. At the Flamingo, Michael is on reception with a busy day of checking in guests, while the maintenance team deals with a burst pipe. Entertainment is key to Halloween and Shari is frantically getting the decorations up, while youngster Tom is entertaining the guests

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare the perfect meal for a special night in, with a starter of scallops served with crispy bacon and a cauliflower puree. The main course is roast chicken stuffed with truffle butter with dauphinoise potatoes and charred cabbage, followed by and a chocolate and almond tart with amaretti biscuits for dessert

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The Red Arrows fly over the airport during the Queens Jubilee celebrations, but Passenger Experience Manager Suki has to wake a passenger and get him onboard a flight to Toronto. Also racing to catch their flight are a mother and daughter flying to South Africa to attend a close family relatives funeral. Emotions run high as time ticks away without the correct paperwork, so its down to Passenger Experience Manager Harry to come to the rescue - but is it already too late?

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: An emergency call comes in to the stroke unit at Bronglais Hospital, Aberystwyth. Issie is becoming more confident in helping stroke patients when they arrive, but is quickly reminded she has a lot to learn and experience to gain. A man is blue-lighted in to AandE after being knocked off his bike, while Caitlin deals with a patient who has been admitted after having seizures

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges. Bizarre new game Flush or Float is showcased, before the host takes a trip to Milton Keynes to give away £1,000 via In for a Penny, In for a Grand

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Jon Sopel, Maisie Summers-Newton, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals and the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in SOS: Survive or Surrender, hosted by former commando Jason Fox, who is in on the whole thing. Actor and comedian Guz Khan is the guest saboteur

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert feels like he has been left with only one option, while Jacob is relieved, and Kyle goes missing

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of Englands most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where six hopefuls compete for £1 million. However, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Ryan mops up disco spills at the 3 Arena and Brandon hopes his cleaning content will bring social media stardom. Wheelie bin cleaner Barry tackles one of Irelands dirtiest jobs

programme.title: Me and My Tourettes
programme.desc: Documentary following three people with Tourettes syndrome as they reach turning points in their lives at a camp that could change everything for them and their families

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Big Interview
programme.desc: Colette Fitzpatrick meets Kathleen Chada, whose husband murdered her young sons Eoghan and Ruairi in 2013

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Adrian Dunbar, Jo Brand, Layton Williams and their relatives compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Living with Lucy
programme.desc: Showband veteran Dickie Rock hosts Lucy Kennedy and talks about his past as well as his plans for the future. Also, Paddy Cole and Sil Fox join the two for a round of golf

programme.title: Body in the Bin: The Murder of Louise Kam
programme.desc: What began as an audacious plot to defraud £5million from unsuspecting 71-year-old mother and businesswoman Louise Kam ended in her murder, after which her body was callously dumped in a wheelie bin on a driveway belonging to one of the killers. This documentary follows this shocking story of fraud, murder and the impact of social media

programme.title: The Night Caller
programme.desc: A vengeful Tony creeps into Lawrences house to face him. Lawrence thinks hes being burgled, until he recognises the intruder as the cabbie who picked him up earlier that night. He heads for the kitchen to call the police but theres a scuffle. Tony grabs a kitchen knife and Lawrence is forced to do what he says. Thriller, starring Robert Glenister

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jennys behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victims widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesnt count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jennys life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Affair at the Victory Ball
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friends estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon is devastated, while Cain is rocked and Kerry is riddled with guilt

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona has a big decision to make, while Cain confides in Moira and Kerry is furious

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain is involved in an accident, while Jacob is left humiliated and Kerry confides in Jai

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kyle finds Cain lying unconscious, while Kerry starts to have suspicions and Moira is concerned

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kerry wades in, while Robert gets an idea and Pollard is left with food for thought

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert decides it is time to come clean, while Dawn is shocked and Kerry apologises

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans directs this episode, set against the backdrop of the first moon landings in 1969. The detective investigates the deaths of an astrophysicist and his girlfriend in a tragic car accident as the rest of the world eagerly awaits news from Apollo 11. When he begins to suspect foul play, Endeavour enlists Thursdays help in getting to the truth, visiting the observatory where the dead man worked and discovering the possibility that he was not the intended victim. The case is complicated further when the dead scientist is revealed to have been part of a mysterious cult-like group

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: The Michelin-starred chef explores new possibilities at his East Sussex smallholding, heading off to the Knepp in Horsham to learn all about different tomato varieties in its impressive market garden. After losing some of his ducks to foxes, Marcus is determined to keep the rest safe and local expert Tom. has interesting and unexpected ideas, including introducing some noisy new arrivals

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the sun rises on the Costa Blanca, the Hotel Benidorm Plazas housekeeping staff spring into action. Meanwhile, expat Jessie has an audition to be the new hotel entertainer, and cameras follow the Parrot family as they head to Benidorm to spend some precious family time together

programme.title: The Secret Life of Our Pets
programme.desc: How domesticated animals use their senses to navigate the human world, from dogs with thermal sensing noses and cats with the ability to navigate using magnetic forces, to masses of flocking birds who use their senses to fly within millimetres from one another. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson helps sisters Sharon and Joy with their struggling country house hotel in Worlington, Suffolk. While the Grade-II listed building is in good repair, the operating systems are chaotic and the menu too extensive for the solitary chef to handle. Ruth organises a revamp of the dated-looking function room - prompting the delighted owners to plan a wedding fair in their new-look space

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Workers digging up a path by Hove lagoon unearth a dark secret that was meant to stay buried for good. Meanwhile, what appears to be an isolated missing-persons incident across the city soon becomes an investigation bigger than Grace could have imagined when a chilling detail linking the lagoon discovery and the missing-person case leads to a hunt for a serial offender. Crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: To Die For (1995)
programme.desc: An ambitious small town woman has a lifelong obsession of becoming a TV star. A job at a local TV station seems like the first step on her journey to the top, but when her husband announces his desire to start a family, she realises he has to go and persuades her dim-witted teenage lover to murder him. Gus Van Sants black comedy, starring Nicole Kidman, Matt Dillon and Joaquin Phoenix

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: The unit investigates the links between a crime lord and a deranged artist who may have been using his skills to create something illegal. Meanwhile, Atwater and Burgess are assigned the seemingly routine task of dealing with a hoarder whose collection is upsetting some of her neighbours - but they discover something shocking amid the clutter. Crime drama, starring Jason Beghe and Sophia Bush

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew OKeefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernons plans for Liz and Bens wedding in chaos, while Helens dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Veteran singer and keyboard player Alan Price guest stars in this festive episode of the rural drama, as the leader of a jazz band. Travelling to Aidensfield, where theyve been booked by Vernon to play at the villagers Christmas concert, the Rio Trio offer a lift to a girl, unaware she is a single mum taking her baby home to see her parents for the holiday. However, the first the band know about the infant is when the girl disappears, leaving them to look after it. Meanwhile, as the concert gets into full swing, local barmaid Gina takes centre stage, hoping to impress all and sundry with her rendition of Winter Wonderland. Tricia Penrose, Derek Fowlds, Geoffrey Hughes, William Simons and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker is forced to confront his past when he undertakes his own unofficial investigation into a series of violent robberies around Aidensfield. Helen has to come to terms with the fact that her marriage is probably over when Matthews mistress appears in the village, and David finds himself involved in some shady behaviour. Jonathan Kerrigan, Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery and David Lonsdale star

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Egyptian Tomb
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummys curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victims widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ben forces Maggie to reveal that Alan was not his father - but can she keep Jim McDonald a secret? Meanwhile, Debbie encounters Carl upon her release from custody, and Sam reluctantly accepts Wills apology

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ronnie learns of Carls latest ploy to extort cash from Debbie, Ben reels from the paternity revelation, Jodie joins David on his day off, and Hope taunts Will in front of Sam

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Debbie makes her presence felt at the hotel, Sam accuses Will of intimidation, Ben refuses to give Maggie the time of day, and Tim summons the courage to face Trisha

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Summer challenges Debbies version of how Billy died, Will denies Sams allegation that hes still seeing Megan, and Lisa and Carla leave Jodie in charge of Connie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah sneaks round to see Todd while Theo is out, Jodie reveals her mischief to Gemma, Nick and Ben decide to get their sons together, and Ronnie offers Carl an olive branch

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah quizzes Danielle about Theos violent streak, Bernie hears promising news of Mals future plans, George meets an estate agent, and Carl gives Summer a clue to the identity of Billys killer

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future

programme.title: Building Giants
programme.desc: The construction of the Rose Fitzgerald Kennedy Bridge over the River Barrow in Co Kilkenny, which has two spans of more than 750ft and stands on a purpose-built artificial island

programme.title: Robson and Jims Fly-Fishing Adventure
programme.desc: Robson Green and Jim Murray head to Scotland where they fish at the River Deveron before heading to the most famous river of them all: the Spey

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: In Somerset, Ruth Watson visits the three-diamond, eight-bedroom hotel Saxonia in Weston-super-Mare. She suggests to owners Jon and Sandie Harrap that unattractive decor and cluttered, kitschy rooms could explain why customers are no longer flocking to stay

programme.title: Shetland
programme.desc: When Toshs friend Annie and her nine-year-old son Noah dont make it home one night, Tosh persuades DI Calder - now a resident of the Isles - that something is wrong. Ruth tries to talk down a jittery young man holding a shotgun over his brothers dead body

programme.title: Anne
programme.desc: Ten years after the disaster at Hillsborough, Anne refuses to give up her fight to get justice for her son Kevin. Fact-based drama, starring Maxine Peake

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of Englands most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert feels like he has been left with only one option, while Jacob is relieved, and Kyle goes missing

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: The couples attend a charity bash, where Rachel tells Karen she has a terrible secret, while Jenny tries to dispel her social unease in time-honoured fashion, by getting drunk. Unfortunately, she also starts a fist-fight with Davids boss, and rounds things off by liberally coating her designer dress in foam from a fire extinguisher, ensuring everyone has a night to remember. Starring Helen Baxendale, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and James Nesbitt

programme.title: Iain Stirlings CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling presents the game show in which five contestants take on five famous faces - Jennifer Metcalfe, James Argent, Marcel Sommerville, Paisley Billings and Jordan Davies - in a series of challenges based on unusual abilities that the celebrities claim they possess

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: The aftermath of a dramatic kiss-off rocks the masseria to its core. Some girls question whether they made the right decision. One couple judges the decision made by their friend, another continues to battle their communication issues, and a third grapples with nerves and uncertainty

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Emotions run high as the girls return from their dates. One wonders whether she missed her shot, while another struggles with the partner swapping, and a secret crush gets exposed

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio by actor Pierce Brosnan, Severence director and stars Ben Stiller, Adam Scott and Britt Lower, and comedians John Bishop and Judi Love. With a musical performance from AJ Tracey and Jorja Smith

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: Jann and her sister come together after finding out about their mothers diagnosis. Comedy, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: The tour gets put on hold when both Janns mum and her sister have health scares. Comedy, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: A review of the latest matches in Europes premier club competition, plus a look ahead to forthcoming fixtures

programme.title: Britains Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britains Brightest Family. In the fifth episode, the Baker family from Wales and the Sheth family from London battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary expert Frances Atkins tests three home cooks who are tasked with making a German pasta called spaetzle. Sheree Murphy hosts

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Pop star and 911 lead singer Lee Brennan prepares a carefully selected menu for a dinner party, with each dish designed to provide his oblivious guests with clues relating to their hosts identity. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Flint family from Hertfordshire swap homes, budgets and lives with the Elayadi family from Ealing, and, though they live just 13 miles away from each other, their financial circumstances are worlds apart. The Elayadis live in a two-bedroom council flat together with their three children and their weekly budget after bills is around £135 per week, while Sam and Richard Flint are among the richest 10 per cent in Britain

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Ryans take on Ladies Day at the Galway Races, Car Lovers restore a 21-year-old vehicle to its former glory and Lukes window cleaning team pursue the perfect streak-free shine

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 4.40pm). Coverage of the final match in the opening round of fixtures, held at Principality Stadium. Scotland claimed a narrow 24-21 home win when the teams met in this competition last year, as well as a more convincing 38-8 victory in Salford in the World Cup last August. The Scots also prevailed 20-18 on Welsh soil in 2024, but Sean Lynns side will be eager to end that run of defeats this time around

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern is in Sheffield as he hosts the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Mark Charnock, Kerry Godliman and Janet Street-Porter all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: A Sheffield pawnbroker takes a helicopter ride to the biggest deal of his career - and sees another chance to make a profit when a customer brings in a Rolex she bought for £25 in a car boot sale. John Freeze faces a tough choice when offered a collection of 20 designer handbags, knowing that the fickle nature of fashion can make their value highly changeable. Ray takes delivery of a bespoke, hand-made Rolls Royce - but with the wedding industry it was made for at a standstill due to Covid-19, buyers may be in short supply

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to revisit some of the most memorable moments from Gogglebox Ireland, demonstrating why Irelands resident telly addicts have developed a reputation for eccentricity. Includes highlights featuring the Tully twins, Angela and Eileen, Anita and Tracey, the Adenugas and the Ryans

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as contestants Daniel, Rebekah, Raymond, Samuel and Daniella face the six Chasers - the Sinnerman, the Beast, the Governess, the Menace, the Vixen and the Dark Destroyer, arguably the greatest quiz team in the world

programme.title: Dark Side of Reality TV
programme.desc: Twenty women are deceived into believing their would-be suitor is worth millions, and made to look like gold diggers in front of 70 million viewers

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: It all comes to a head as old adversaries clash, with the fate of New Eden hanging in the balance

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony-winning actor Viola Davis, star of Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 Will Poulter and renowned comedian Jack Dee. Plus, a performance from Simple Minds of their iconic hit Dont You (Forget About Me) ahead of the songs 40th anniversary

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: After a disastrous tour with Sarah McLachlan, Jann returns home, only to discover everyone in her life is mad at her. Canadian comedy series, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: A performance at a charity gala gives Jann the chance to win back her ex-girlfriend. Canadian comedy, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz show in which contestants use their wits to get through to the final, where the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the amount of money they play for. Which of todays nine - Don, Emma, Nicky, Tom, Tracey, Beth, Sue, Jonathan and Shane - will be playing for the jackpot?

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Culinary challenge hosted by Sheree Murphy in which michelin starred chef Rupert Rowley puts three home cooks through a series of challenges, including how to make a crepe suzette

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Designer Linda Barker puts on a dinner party for four strangers, who have to work out the the identity of their mystery celebrity host with the help of a few cryptic clues - otherwise they will be doing the washing up. Hosted by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is shocked to discover her half-brother Frank is planning to marry a woman more than twice his age. Chandler tries to stop smoking with the aid of hypnosis, only to suffer strange side-effects, and Monica gets a handy tip that could net her a small fortune

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Monica steals the heart of a millionaire, but Rosss spirits plummet thanks to Rachel, who dates his arch-rival Mark. Meanwhile, Joey lands a part in a new play, but clashes with his female co-star. Guest starring Iron Man director Jon Favreau

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey meets Miss Right, and Rachel is dismayed to learn her boss has started dating Chandler. Meanwhile, Monica inherits a dolls house, filling Phoebe with jealousy

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: Heartbroken Ross makes a sacrifice for Rachel, Monica faces a difficult decision over a career opportunity and Chandler is surprised by Joeys strange choice of gift

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey proves a hit with his co-star, despite what the critics think. Rachel dates a stranger who seems outwardly charming - until he starts screaming at people. Unfortunately, only Ross witnesses his bizarre behaviour and nobody else believes him. Guest starring Ben Stiller

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross discovers a strange growth on his buttocks, Phoebe finds it hard to choose between two prospective lovers, and Monicas boyfriend surprises her. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: The weekly final sees chefs Galton Blackiston, Frances Atkins, Rupert Rowley and Theo Randall pair up with their chosen home cook partners and go head to head in the ultimate pro-am cooking competition. And to pile on the pressure, three-star Michelin chef Pierre Koffmann judges their final dishes. Culinary challenge, hosted by Sheree Murphy

programme.title: The Voice USA
programme.desc: Carson Daly presents as Kelly Clarkson, John Legend and Adam Levine continue the blind auditions, as all three of them build their teams for this champion of champions special

programme.title: Womens Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of fixtures, featuring England v Ireland at Allianz Stadium Twickenham, Wales v Scotland at Principality Stadium and France v Italy at Stade des Alpes

programme.title: Chasing Famous
programme.desc: An intimate and unfiltered dive into one of Generation Zs most lucrative and sought after professions

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by reality star and businesswoman Kim Kardashian and award-winning actress Sarah Paulson, who are appearing together in the new TV legal drama Alls Fair. Breaking Bad star Bryan Cranston chats about taking to the West End stage in All My Sons, while actress and singer Rachel Zegler also appears, fresh from wowing audiences at the London Palladium in Evita. Plus, Little Simz performs Lion, from her album Lotus

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Oscar-nominated star of Schindlers List, Ralph Fiennes, who is on the sofa to talk about his play Grace Pervades. Comedy actress Anna Faris returns to the world of Scary Movie for the next instalment in the spoof-horror franchise. Olivia Cooke, star of Amazon Primes The Girlfriend, talks about the upcoming series in the Game of Thrones prequel, House of the Dragon, and comedian Michelle de Swarte tells Claudia about her stand-up tour The Afters

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Dannii Minogue gets the women back together eight months on from the end of the show to unpick the drama and find out all the gossip since their summer in Italy. Whose love has lasted the distance? And has anyone made any shocking U-turns?

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Life within a specialist clinic, where a team of top dermatologists treats a range of skin conditions for patients who often havent been able to get treatment on the NHS

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: The women near their final -kiss-off. Will they commit to a relationship back home or call time on their summer romance? Hosted by Dannii Minogue
